programme.desc: Clean, sanitise, and help deodorise your home using just simple kitchen ingredients and electricity. The H2O e3 supercharges water with the salt you eat to create a multi purpose super cleaner!

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella question the employees of a dock worker whose crushed remains have been found on Staten Island, before being led to another corpse - but suspects for both murders are thin on the ground. Meanwhile, an apparent hit-and-run turns into a complex puzzle for Danny and Aiden. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise, Melina Kanakaredes, Carmine Giovinazzo and Vanessa Ferlito

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella have reason to suspect a police officer is involved in the murder of a wine store owner after evidence links him to a Hispanic gang present at the crime scene. Their investigations prove upsetting for Flack, as the man under suspicion is his old mentor

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Dan Stanwyck, an investigator working on a report into police corruption, is fatally shot the night before his findings are published. Taylor and Stella are convinced he was killed by a crooked officer whose wrongdoings were about to be exposed. Meanwhile, Danny and Aiden look into the case of a taxi driver stabbed to death in his cab

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team enlists a former secret service agent to help try to prevent a nuclear attack on an American ally, after an attempted hacker tries to launch a weapon of mass destruction. Meanwhile, Paige considers staying in Los Angeles or moving 3,000 miles away to Portland, Maine, so Ralph can attend an exclusive school. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team searches for stolen experimental shoulder-launch rockets, which are rumoured to being transported on a luxury cruise ship. Meanwhile, Ralph impresses his first crush with the help of Happy and Toby. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Tony reviews satellite footage of rebel forces in the Middle East and spots Kersey among a group of insurgents, concluding that Joe Westcott's fear of him was well-founded. Back at HQ, Abby creates a 3D computer model of the attack that occurred in Afghanistan and Joe dons a virtual-reality headset to relive the traumatic events. Will it jog his memory?

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Teenager Lydia Wade manages to escape a gang of masked men trying to abduct her and her friend Rosie, the daughter of a US Navy commander. Lydia's Navy captain father David Wade was shot dead during the attack and her mother is overseas, so Ziva offers to look after the girl, while Abby tries to jog her memory by creating facial reconstructions of the attackers based on some grainy CCTV footage. DiNozzo and McGee find links on Rosie's laptop to possible suspect John 'Diesel' Stevens

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is called to the scene when there is an attempt on the life of FBI agent TC Fornell, with the assailant identified as Navy seaman Tyler Brown. The pair team up and visit the bar where Brown worked and bump into their mutual ex-wife Diane Sterling, who was operating undercover as an employee suspected of stealing customers' identities. Joe Spano and Melinda McGraw guest star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Returning from a six-month posting in the Gulf, Navy lieutenant commander Megan Huffner opens her front door to find her husband Noel dead in his chair. Gibbs and the team investigate and find the victim died three or four weeks earlier, and an unusual $100 bill in his wallet proves to be a vital clue. Meanwhile, Tony's father visits for Christmas and looks to rebuild their fractured relationship. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. A man and his son on a fishing trip hook what appears to be the body of a petty officer, but the dead man turns out to be a journalist who had been investigating the proliferation of drugs in the Navy. Meanwhile, Ziva returns to her car after a workout at the gym to find her father Eli hiding inside. Guest starring Michael Nouri

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team enlists a former secret service agent to help try to prevent a nuclear attack on an American ally, after an attempted hacker tries to launch a weapon of mass destruction. Meanwhile, Paige considers staying in Los Angeles or moving 3,000 miles away to Portland, Maine, so Ralph can attend an exclusive school. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team searches for stolen experimental shoulder-launch rockets, which are rumoured to being transported on a luxury cruise ship. Meanwhile, Ralph impresses his first crush with the help of Happy and Toby. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The notorious Tri-State Sniper appears to have claimed another victim when a petty officer is shot dead in a crowded park. However, when the team discovers the killer in question was already in custody on the day of the shooting, it becomes clear a copy-cat is on the loose. As their investigation progresses, the agents learn the victim was recently found guilty of placing a camera in the female changing rooms of the USS Camden, earning himself plenty of enemies - but did any of them hate him enough to kill?

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called to investigate the murder of a retired US Navy lieutenant commander, and the inquiry forces Ducky to reveal he has been working as part of a secret society dedicated to solving cold cases. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo is reunited with his old flame, Jeanne Benoit, when insurgents in Sudan attack a group of volunteer doctors, including her husband. Travelling to the crime scene to conduct a joint murder-kidnapping investigation, Dinozzi and McGee offer their assistance in searching for the missing doctors. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Sean Murray, with Scottie Thompson

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Petty officer Kyle Friedgen is accused of killing his ex-girlfriend, but while his case is dismissed due to a mishandled warrant, his life and reputation are marred by suspicion that he got away with murder. Determined to clear his name in full, Friedgen volunteers to be court-martialed, on the condition that the NCIS team conducts its own investigation. Meanwhile, Bishop and Jake discuss their marital problems. Naval crime drama, starring Emily Wickersham, with Jamie Bamber

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The two Marine brothers of a sailor battling leukaemia are killed in the line of duty, and to spare the servicemen's family further grief, the secretary of the US Navy seeks the NCIS team's help in locating a suitable bone marrow donor. Meanwhile, Bishop returns home to Oklahoma for Thanksgiving. Military crime drama, starring Emily Wickersham

To her surprise, he initially appears to be a good Samaritan who witnessed a robbery and is fully co-operating, but a stake-out leads her to believe otherwise - and she soon learns a shocking fact about her vision

programme.desc: Part two of two. The authorities continue their investigation into the death of Lt Angela Arutti, while the aircraft carrier remains on alert. David James Elliott and John M Jackson star

programme.desc: Sloan's birthday turns out to be less than festive when he discovers the dead body of a prominent divorce lawyer in the hospital pharmacy. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part story, Lewis sinks deeper into trouble as his undercover operation spins out of control. Following a shoot-out with the East Street gang, he is ordered to act as courier when a hostage is exchanged for a gun the Skens are eager to get their hands on - and finds he is in danger from both sides. Back at the station, Mickey and Kezia work on a case in which explosives have gone missing from a contractor's depot. Concludes tomorrow

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: In the tense conclusion of the two-part story, Heaton briefs the team on the Skens' plan to ambush a cash van, but Lewis alerts them to a last-minute change of plan - the stolen explosives are to be used to rescue gang leader Wayne Jackson from a prison vehicle on the courthouse run. A high-speed car chase through the London streets ensues, observed from the air by Jack until the getaway car enters a tunnel. The trail goes cold, leaving Lewis in the hands of the increasingly suspicious gangsters

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Darren Day guest stars as a photographer arrested on suspicion of murdering one of his models. The discovery of a blood-soaked T-shirt at his trashed studio, and the evidence of his own pictures, suggests a second suspect may have delivered the fatal blows to the victim's head. Emma and Beth tangle with a trio of truculent young women whose troubled history of deceit and blackmail leads to violence, putting the young PCs in danger

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: CID officers attend the funeral of murdered supermodel Cindy Statham, only to learn that killer Larry Franks has been transferred to hospital following a suicide bid. When he makes a break for freedom, a frantic search of the building ensues and Beth is first to pick up the trail of blood. Smithy's first day as Acting Inspector goes from bad to worse when he calls in the Firearms Command to sort out a building society robbery by two bungling ex-employees

programme.title: Emma
programme.desc: Bafta award-winning writer Sandy Welch's dramatisation of Jane Austen's novel, first published in 1815. Romola Garai stars as the independent Emma Woodhouse, who believes she is a skilled matchmaker and tries to pair up her friends and acquaintances. She takes Harriet Smith under her wing and encourages the woman to set her sights higher than her current suitor - against Mr Knightley's warnings about the dangers of meddling. With Jonny Lee Miller, Michael Gambon, Jodhi May and Tamsin Greig

programme.title: Emma
programme.desc: While continuing her efforts to set up Harriet and the vicar, Emma looks forward to meeting Frank Churchill at the Christmas party, but when he fails to show up she becomes the subject of Mr Elton's unwanted attention. Later, village gossip focuses on the arrival of Jane Fairfax and a piano she was given by a mystery admirer, leaving the matchmaker reluctant to believe Mr Knightley could have sent the gift. Drama, starring Romola Garai, Louise Dylan, Blake Ritson and Laura Pyper

programme.title: Emma
programme.desc: The heroine plans a ball with Frank, and wonders whether she has feelings for him. Despite having a wonderful time at the event, she still feels trapped, and with the matchmaking effort officially abandoned, becomes increasingly bored. However, her spirits are raised by Mr Knightley's proposal for a group outing to Box Hill, where he tells her he thinks Frank and Jane are in love. Drama, starring Romola Garai and Michael Gambon

programme.title: Emma
programme.desc: The outing to Box Hill ends in disaster when Emma insults Miss Bates. After being berated by Knightley, she realises her behaviour was shameful and tries to make amends, but Jane refuses to speak to her. When Frank's overbearing aunt dies, the Westons expect him to propose to Emma, but his subsequent actions start a chain of events that shocks the heroine, resulting in the realisation of a truth she previously dismissed. Romola Garai and Jonny Lee Miller star in Sandy Welch's dramatisation of Jane Austen's novel

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's Tilly Trotter
programme.desc: Concluding part. Following the destruction of their home by the McGraths, Tilly and her grandmother are forced to live a life of solitude. Tilly and Katie Drew begin working at Sopwith's mine. Despite the hardships of life in the pit, she enjoys feeling like part of the family. Period drama, starring Carli Norris and Sarah Jane Foster

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne receives an SOS from Holloway Prison, whose usual maternity workers have been struck down by illness, and when she and Trixie step in to help, the nun puts her reputation on the line to prevent a heavily pregnant convict having her unborn baby taken away by social services. Shelagh faces heartache when a routine pregnancy check reveals something far more serious, Alec has a confession for Jenny, and Fred takes the gang to the theatre to celebrate Chummy's birthday - only for the trip to end in disaster. Lacey Turner, best known as EastEnders' Stacey Branning, guest stars

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Jenny has an eventful few days, starting with a patient who as well as having herself to worry about, is also struggling to cope with her mother, an agoraphobic who hasn't been outside for at least 12 years. On top of that, Alec asks the midwife to accompany him to Brighton for the weekend, leaving her wondering if his intentions are strictly honourable. However, things soon take a tragic turn. Meanwhile, Shelagh joins the local choir to take her mind off her problems. Jessica Raine, Leo Staar and Laura Main star

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A young nun is found dead in a smoke-filled bedroom in what seems like a terrible accident caused by a lit cigarette. But when Richard deduces it must have been murder, there are no shortage of suspects, including other nuns, a priest and the mother superior - as well as an unscrupulous PR man promoting a holy spring in the convent grounds. The question is, with the room locked from the inside, how could anyone have committed the crime in the first place? Kenneth Cranham, Patrick Baladi and Caroline Langrishe guest star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: The body of a missing teenager is found and ambitious DCI Claire Ashby is committed to proving the guilt of her main suspect, a local locksmith

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Hugh finds an unlikely test subject for his revolutionary new artificial heart. Starring Rodger Corser, Nicole da Silva and Ryan Johnson

programme.title: The Heart Guy

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: The Glaswegian detective is drawn into a suicide case where all is not as it seems - despite his usual attempts to stay out of such investigations. With former Brookside actor Steven Pinder

Guest starring McFly

programme.desc: Concern grows for Jo's safety when she fails to show up for work and further study of the videotape made by Larry Franks yields vital clues to the killer's identity. programme.desc: Garry opens his stall and forces the other market traders to do the same. Kathy helps Pat reach a decision. Meanwhile, Cindy's not enthusiastic about Ian's property hunting trip

programme.desc: Pauline tells the truth about the prison visits. Tiffany's cross with Tony for cancelling their date. Bianca makes peace with Carol, but when Ricky proposes she turns him down

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James wins the football pools, and Siegfried visits a farm to treat a sick cat and pulls off a brilliant piece of human diagnosis. Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Lynda Bellingham star

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Arthur Smith makes his annual visit to Grantleigh, but as he happens to be a tramp, DeVere becomes concerned about his presence. Guest starring Bill Travers (Born Free)

programme.desc: The staff discover their freedom of choice regarding holidays is under threat when the store closes for redecorating - prompting a typical response from the shop floor. Classic comedy, starring Mollie Sugden, Trevor Bannister and John Inman

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Hobbo's quest to find a man for Stella leads him to put up an advertisement, but the suitor who turns up lacks what it takes to tackle the widow and runs off. However, a chance to hit back emerges when Hobbo finds out who Stella's favourite movie character is

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: When Hobbo learns that Howard feels he is taken for granted by Pearl and that Toby misses his former wife, he hatches a plan to sort out their love lives - with the help of flowers, a Mario Lanza recording and his own team of miming violinists

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: Ellen Mallam is left to drown in an upturned car by its driver, but the young woman's connection to James Blackstone, an aristocratic family's heir apparent, impedes Gently and Bacchus's investigation. The chief inspector discovers that the son's future has been carefully mapped out since birth by his implacable mother Alethea, and she will not countenance any interference in her plans. However, an astonishing twist causes the detectives to reconsider the case in its entirety. Sixties-set detective drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby, with Geraldine Somerville, James Norton, Roger Lloyd Pack, Christopher Fairbank and Ebony Buckle

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Jack examines a case previously undertaken by a deceased colleague, who was convinced that the alleged suicide of a journalist was a cover-up by the American government. Enlisting the help of a UFO-spotter, the team discovers the reporter witnessed a mysterious air crash near a US Air Force base, and the retired officers try to track down the original personnel to get some answers. Guest starring Mark Williams, Glynis Barber, Michael Brandon and Raquel Cassidy, with Amanda Redman

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Boyd investigates a disturbing case of abuse and murder at a children's home as new evidence comes to light about the disappearance of two boys. When an artist's impression of a skeleton found in an archaeological dig matches one of the youngsters, the team tries to determine the identity of the paedophile. Starring Trevor Eve, Sue Johnston and Claire Goose

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Preparations for his first Christmas with Holly prompt Linden to finally face up to his guilt over his wife's death, as the words of a dying priest lead him to tell her the truth about the tragedy. Chrissie announces she is pregnant but will not identify the father, while Donna reunites a mother with her missing daughter, but is so busy she neglects to inform her colleagues of the arrangements for the party she has been organising. Daisha enjoys Oliver's company before leaving for the Philippines

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Comfort overdoes it and ends up being rushed into Holby suffering severe stomach pains. Abs grows concerned about Ellen's well-being when he learns she's had a secret meeting with her father, but gets a shock when he comes face to face with Gustav at the flat. Meanwhile, Bex organises Charlie's 50th birthday party and finds herself sharing a passionate kiss with Claire's brother Pete

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: New recruit Sergeant Callum Stone (Sam Callis) makes an instant impact at Sun Hill by subduing a violent drunk in the station, but his maverick methods instantly alarm Dan and raise Smithy's hackles. Terry traces a series of threatening calls made to elderly women back to a single suspect, a supervisor at a kitchen fitting company, who is less than pleased to be arrested in a lift stalled between floors. Michael Angelis guest stars

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany tries to keep Tony out of trouble, while Kathy has a request for Pat. Meanwhile, Mark gets a shock when he visits Pauline

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Arrangements for Ben's christening create problems for Kathy and Phil. Grant is not interested in the Lonely Heart ad replies - but someone else is

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Major Cochrane is charged with the attempted rape of the daughter of an influential businessman. Elsewhere, rivalry surfaces between the men and women during a dragon boat race

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Crabbe discovers a customer's body in the men's toilet, and its subsequent disappearance makes great demands on his ingenuity. Light-hearted crime drama, starring Richard Griffiths, Maggie Steed and Bella Enahoro

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James gets caught in a feud between Oliver and Roland Strong, who have not spoken to each other for 50 years. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Audrey, Marjory and their Nature Conservancy friends try to foil DeVere's plans to change the countryside - but Mrs fforbes-Hamilton quickly changes allegiance when their plot is unsuccessful. Comedy, starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The staff wonder who will benefit from an opportunity for promotion to the board, and Mr Humphries reveals his clairvoyant abilities. Shop-floor comedy, with John Inman, Mollie Sugden and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Convinced that Nelly is his mother, Hobbo sends Howard to eavesdrop on her and Pearl as he tries to find out more about his father. However, when the women concoct some false information, the police start to take an interest in the men's activities. Comedy, starring Russ Abbot, June Whitfield and Robert Fyfe

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Hobbo discovers that an infirm friend needs help to bury his pet in the churchyard, and calls on Alvin and Entwistle to carry out the deed. However, the mission becomes complicated when the police begin to suspect the trio are up to no good. Comedy, starring Russ Abbot, Burt Kwouk and Brian Murphy

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detective and Havers are sent to the Scottish Highlands to investigate the murder of a respected playwright, only to be confronted by glamorous stars from the film industry. As events unfold, they discover sexual jealousy, unstable relationships and numerous lies behind the glossy showbiz facade. Detective drama, guest starring James McAvoy, with Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Gerry and the team agree to help a man who believes his wife is still alive, even though she apparently died 18 months earlier in a car accident. They decide the best way to convince him is to have her exhumed - only to be faced with more questions than answers when it turns out the body is not hers after all. Meanwhile, Brian needs to find a handyman when Esther bans him from his latest DIY project. Hugh Dennis and Samuel West guest star, with Amanda Redman

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The team discovers a link between a mummified body and a Notting Hill house that featured as the location for a murderous fight between gangsters in a 1960s film, and present-day renovation work reveals more skeletons behind the walls. Spencer complicates matters further by revealing he lived there with his family when the events must have taken place. Crime drama, starring Trevor Eve, Sue Johnston and Holly Aird

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: New recruit Sergeant Callum Stone (Sam Callis) makes an instant impact at Sun Hill by subduing a violent drunk in the station, but his maverick methods instantly alarm Dan and raise Smithy's hackles. Terry traces a series of threatening calls made to elderly women back to a single suspect, a supervisor at a kitchen fitting company, who is less than pleased to be arrested in a lift stalled between floors. programme.desc: Willem and Anna investigate the death of a project developer to find out about Couwenberg's involvment, but Iris is uncomfortable with their relationship

programme.title: Cymry ar Gynfas
programme.desc: Mixed-media artist Luned Rhys Parri creates a portrait of musician, radio presenter and archaeologist Rhys Mwyn

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Leicester City v Southampton at King Power Stadium and Wolverhampton Wanderers v West Bromwich Albion at Molineux. The Saints beat Leicester 2-1 here last term to gain a measure of revenge for a stunning 9-0 hammering in the reverse fixture. The first Black Country derby took place today since the Baggies prevailed 5-1 here in the 2011/12 season. Plus, Leeds United v Brighton & Hove Albion, West Ham United v Burnley and Aston Villa v Everton

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew's guests are Sir Simon Stevens, chief executive of NHS England, Dominic Raab MP, foreign secretary, Ed Davey MP, leader of the Liberal Democrats, Gloria Borger, CNN chief political analyst. News review with Hugh Pym, BBC health editor and Lucy Fisher, deputy political editor, Daily Telegraph

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell returns with the programme that features moral and religious discussions on topical issues

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Kiwi-born Felicity stayed in the UK to raise her daughter, but now wants to relocate to Australia. However, daughter Lucy says she doesn't want to go, so the pair sample a week 'Down Under'. At the end of the trial, Lucy has to vote on where her future lies

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant help two teams at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, Powys, while Charlie Ross finds out about a collection of vintage film posters

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Singer and new presenter Brenda Edwards visits an outdoor walking church in east London, while Katie Piper meets a vicar who has become a hit on social media with 60-second sermons

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple hoping to relocate to rural Somerset on a budget of £700,000, looking for a home with enough land for animals and a glamping site. Away from the hunt, Alistair gets stuck in with some Cheddar making in the county where it originated

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore saves three things about to be dumped at a tip, transforms them into bespoke and valuable pieces, before selling them and giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of them in the first place

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to West Bromwich, where Pauline recently bought a house complete with her very first garden. However, she has no idea what to do with the plot, which is currently full of weeds and random bits of old broken junk

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: Examining the solar force that powers Earth and how plants and animals have adapted to the uneven amounts of sunlight given to different parts of the planet over the course of a year. The exception is on the equator, where the duration of day and night remain the same throughout the year. Here, this guaranteed sunlight drives the great diversity of life in the tropical jungles

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Tom Heap head to Somerset to visit the Mendips to find out how this stunning but fragile area is being protected from the invasion of visitors. Ellie scales Cheddar Gorge as well as discovering the damage cars are wreaking on narrow winding roads, and how white-clawed freshwater crayfish are being saved from voracious invaders. Tom finds out how the countryside can help transform lives and also investigates if the government's new green subsidy scheme can really deliver for farmers. Adam Henson meets ex-international rugby union ref turned Nigel Owens, who is now a farmer and facing his herd's first TB test

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Christchurch Park in Ipswich, where items assessed include an heirloom Rolex, memorabilia related to Donald Campbell's land-speed record and a necklace worth half a million pounds. There is also a rare Roman relic dug up in the garden and a pocket watch with a hidden secret

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at how the uneven distribution of freshwater around the globe has given rise to an incredible diversity of species and habits, from the driest desert to the lushest tropical rainforest. Featuring a colony of ants banding together into a raft every time its home in the Amazon floods, a rain frog that manages to eke out an existence in one of the world's driest habitats on Earth, and the last wild camels that survive the Gobi Desert's bitter winters by eating snow that blows in from Siberia

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Charles, Marie-Andree and Ajay flee Bangkok for Nepal, where they scour the streets for victims, but tensions arise when Charles loses the group's money in a card game until Marie-Andree overcomes her crisis of conscience to deliver their next prey. In March 1976, Nadine helps Herman and Angela to accumulate evidence by taking undercover photos of the suspects and their private apartment

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Liverpool v Manchester United, Sheffield United v Tottenham Hotspur and Manchester City v Crystal Palace. The Reds won last season's corresponding fixture 2-0 en route to winning the title and another such result would further cement their status as title-favourites. Meanwhile, Spurs and City will have expected victories to boost their own chances of success. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Kelly Somers presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Manchester United, Manchester City v Aston Villa and Reading v Arsenal

programme.title: All the President's Men (1976)
programme.desc: The true story of Washington Post reporters Bob Woodward and Carl Bernstein, who persevered in their investigation of the links between the Watergate break-in and the White House, a scandal that eventually forced the resignation of President Nixon. Alan J Pakula's drama, starring Robert Redford, Dustin Hoffman, Jason Robards and Hal Holbrook

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Angela Rippon and Gloria Hunniford return to investigate more consumer issues, this week looking at those affecting people during lockdown. They begin with reports on the scammers capitalising on the pandemic, including criminals targeting those waiting for vaccines

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell check out a flat in Southfields, south-west London, a maisonette in Wolverhampton and a three-floor terrace house in Ashton-under-Lyne in Greater Manchester

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with a couple who sampled a week in Melbourne, when Nat Baker hoped to sway his sceptical wife Gemma into making the move to Australia

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A distinctive sticker on a laptop finally links a man to a string of burglaries that had terrified a community, and shiny copper hair dye puts a dangerous armed robber behind bars

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Raj Bisram help two teams look for items at Bath Racecourse, while presenter Eric Knowles look at Chinese ceramics with an eye-watering price tag

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A mysterious saboteur tries to stop Kembleford winning the Three Counties choir competition. Return of the 1950s-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams as the Roman Catholic priest who attempts to solve various misdemeanours

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare indulgent treats for when family and friends come on over - a baked cheesecake, a crowd-pleasing terrine and the ultimate beef sandwich

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown helps a couple from Buckinghamshire who are looking for a detached property in Rutland with a garden big enough for a vegetable patch, all on their budget of £500,000. Continuing the horticultural theme, Steve visits Barnsdale Gardens to see for himself the legacy left by Geoff Hamilton, one of the country's best-loved gardeners

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Jeff and daughters Tracy and Karen take their honey to Bowhouse Farmers' Market in Fife, where organic vegetable growers Connie and Tom also try to sell their produce

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against one another to buy a collection of curiosities, an unusual chair, a mask, candlesticks from a famous maker, and a writing slope. Nigel Havers presents

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones hosts first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Mark Daly investigates why black men in the UK are more likely than white men to have force used on them by police, and to die in police custody. He follows the family of Kevin Clarke on their search for justice after Mr Clarke repeatedly said 'I can't breathe' as he was restrained by police on the ground for 14 minutes during a mental health crisis, and died soon afterwards. The programme also reveals fresh evidence in Scotland's most high-profile death in custody after Sheku Bayoh died in 2015 after being restrained by up to six officers

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda suggests going out for Mick's birthday, but he is more concerned with Tina as she never usually forgets his big day. Frankie pushes Stuart for information on Mick, while Jay is shocked to learn Rainie has been earning extra cash on the side doing house clearances for customers. Max continues to pressure Ian to take a test, and Sharon feels guilty when Kathy tells her she knows her son is unwell

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben and Susan hold their breath as they wait to see if they are expecting a baby, fearful they could produce another one just like Nick. Ben's new assistant Sally puts all his patients off by reacting queasily to the blood that is part and parcel of the job

programme.title: Traces
programme.desc: Emma's accusation against Phil drives a wedge between her and Daniel, while McKinven urges her to stop her own amateur investigation as he becomes certain that the person who killed her mother is known to her

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: David and Michael are butting heads, a situation not helped by having to read with various new potential cast members. They are increasingly annoyed at each new interaction, while Simon tries to keep the ship steady and Georgia marshals her charity team

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The return of the competition in which contestants fight to be crowned the next superstar drag queen, with guest judge Elizabeth Hurley joining Michelle Visage and Graham Norton. In the opening edition, the hopefuls take on their first challenge in the form of a photoshoot with RuPaul, and then are asked to serve two looks on the runway

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Bodyguard and Durrells star Keeley Hawes joins the host to discuss her new drama series Finding Alice, while writer and one-time Doctor Who co-star Noel Clarke talks about his cop drama Bulletproof: South Africa. Hollywood star Rebel Wilson chats about Straight Talking, which sees her collaborate with former soldier Ant Middleton, and eminent film-maker M Night Shyamalan promotes his new psychological horror series Servant. Comedian Daniel Sloss also guests and Jake Bugg performs his current single All I Need

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: With millions of people swapping the gym for the living room during the Covid-19 pandemic, Mehreen Baig delves into the latest science to find the best way to get fit at home. She uncovers exciting new research testing the theory that just one minute of strength training per week on each main muscle group might reduce your risk of diabetes and heart disease, and also looks at the impact wearable fitness tech actually has on our willpower to stick to an exercise plan. Later, she investigates whether popular supplements like pre-trainers and protein shakes really work

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Angela Rippon and Gloria Hunniford investigate issues affecting viewers in lockdown, including poor delivery service from one of the biggest names in the business

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits terraced properties in Grimethorpe, South Yorkshire, and Burnley, Lancashire, while Martel Maxwell is impressed by a basement flat in Bayswater, London

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with the Hagos family two years after they gave life in New Zealand a trial week, and discovers if concerns over their youngest son's welfare were founded

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a con woman's fake claims of having cancer were exposed by her distinctive wallpaper, and the hidden compartment in a car that brought a gang defrauding charities to justice

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning is at Newark Antiques Fair, with experts Tim Weeks and Kate Bliss. Anita also shows off a collection of antiques that are perfect for those with a sweet tooth

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuthing priest investigates the mysterious death of a beekeeper. Nigel Planer guest stars in the 1950s-set murder mystery series

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers create a twist on a classic pudding, rustle up a teatime treat, and use two breakfast favourites to make a new dish

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a family from Berkshire to find a new home in Devon where the children can enjoy the great outdoors, and visits a farm that grows edible flowers

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Mike and grandson Jan prepare for Petersfield market, hoping to sell their cheeses and home-grown veg alongside Phil and Sue, selling their 100-year-old secret-recipe sausages

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents the show in which five dealers bid to buy a retro coat stand, a train set, an unusual chair, some maritime history and a quirky house-clearance find

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A hungover Frankie finds Mick, and despite the tension she wishes him a happy birthday, before revealing she is leaving today. Will he stop her getting on a plane to Australia or let her go? With her conscience is pricking at her, Sharon tells Phil she cannot go through with their plan - so he reassures her he will get something stronger to do the job much more quickly. Iqra has exciting news for her family

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Skylar struggles to forgive herself as the hearing reaches a verdict. Jodie's campaign against Beka puts Sacha's life in danger too, and the only hope is that Lucky can work out what is going on before it's too late. While Jac tries to throw herself into her working day, she is unable to shake her concerns about what Kian is getting up to

programme.title: The Crash Detectives
programme.desc: When a head-on crash leaves a grandfather dead, Chris discovers the answers could lie in electronic data within the car. As his mission to access it begins, he unearths an intriguing story of what really led up to the incident

programme.title: Traces
programme.desc: While Kathy oversees the excavation of Marie's grave, Daniel gives McKinven the lead he needs to make an arrest, but it's the exacting forensic science that finally brings peace for Emma

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: Simon receives desperate news from Georgia, and makes yet another attempt to get David and Michael to work together. However, it ultimately falls to a final pair of new potential cast members to offer the pair a new perspective on their friendship before it is too late

programme.title: BAMOUS
programme.desc: Dane Baptiste hosts a topical comedy show exploring the NASBLAQ - a mythical stock index for black performers - through stand-up routines and sketches. Featuring appearances by a exciting up-and-coming comedians

programme.title: Supercar, Superfam
programme.desc: One-off special catching up with Kash and Shabs in the wake of a disastrous fire, as they make plans to restore the custom supercar business bigger and better than before. Kash is unaware that Sheila is making big plans for their wedding anniversary, and a new client presents the team with a challenge that stretches their skills to the limit

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the cash prize

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Captain Matt Dawson is joined by British athletics stars Adam Gemili and Eilidh Doyle, while Phil Tufnell teams up with former footballers Leon Osman and Stephen Warnock. Sue Barker presents

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at themes beginning with the letter D, as part of her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the vision in squid and owls, revealing how these entirely different creatures have both adapted to life in the dark. He also looks at gorillas and vampire bats both gained unpleasant reputations within the animal kingdom

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Part two of two. Monty Don and the team look back at the horticultural highlights from 2020 and celebrate the very best inspirational gardens and gardeners up and down the country. There is also another chance to see what viewers were getting up to in their gardens throughout the year

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: After the turmoil of 2020 and as a new year gets underway, the team looks at how people can plan to make the countryside a bigger part of their lives? Matt Baker wants to get fitter and tries his hand at cani-cross, Anita Rani straps on her walking boots, Margherita Taylor heads for the woods and Helen Skelton aims to buy local. Tom Heap discovers the joys of 'blue' therapy and Adam Henson gets creative with clay

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick discovers an unusual superstition and a rare type of Norman castle in Launceston, before meeting musicians Graham Fitkin and Ruth Wall in the far west of Cornwall

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate British pork, preparing a traditional gala pie and a rolled loin with apricots and ginger. They also explore the role the pig has played in the UK's history and travel to a farm in Gloucestershire to visit a couple raising rare breeds

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Coverage of the opening session of the final, where the first eight frames are scheduled to take place at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes. Stuart Bingham won the final two frames of this session against Ali Carter last year in London to take a 5-3 lead into the evening. Jason Mohammad presents, with commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the team are at Normanby Hall in north Lincolnshire, where experts Michael Baggot and Caroline Hawley unearth some favourite antiques among the crowd, including a French poster. Plus, Paul uncovers how the building became a hospital during the First World War

programme.title: Ski Sunday
programme.desc: Highlights of the classic Lauberhorn downhill ski races from Flachau in Austria, where Dave Ryding was hoping to improve on his third place finish the previous week

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Coverage of the concluding session of the final at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, where the first player to reach 10 frames will claim the title. Stuart Bingham became the oldest-ever winner of this tournament at the age of 43 when he defeated Ali Carter 10-8 in last year's showpiece match in London, claiming his most prestigious honour since he won the World Championship in 2015. Carter had fought back from a 5-3 deficit at the end of the first session to go into a 7-5 lead, but Bingham was then able to win four consecutive frames to leave him on the verge of victory. Jason Mohammad presents, with commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo

programme.title: Mississippi Grind (2015)
programme.desc: A down-on-his-luck gambler finds his fortunes changing when he makes a new friend, who persuades him to compete in a high-stakes poker game in New Orleans. They embark on a road trip alongside the Mississippi river, taking the opportunity to test their skills at every racetrack, casino and pool hall along the way. Drama, starring Ben Mendelsohn, Ryan Reynolds and Sienna Miller

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the showdown against Arthur, the Petrelli brothers find themselves battling each other - and Nathan makes a move that will have global repercussions. Meanwhile, a face-off ensues when Sylar holds Claire, Noah, Meredith and Angela hostage at Primatech. Ando, Matt and Daphne continue their efforts to rescue Hiro - and Suresh may be their only hope. Starring Milo Ventimiglia, Adrian Pasdar and Hayden Panettiere

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Months after the fall of Pinehurst and Primatech, suresh has gone back to driving taxis, while Daphne and Matt are trying to live as an ordinary couple. But Claire's routine is shaken up when she uncovers Nathan's deadly plot to capture those with abilities - and a powerless Hiro is the first victim when he is dragged away by hooded men. Elsewhere, Sylar searches for his real parents. Guest starring Zeljko Ivanek (Damages)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Skylar's mistake has fatal consequences, but she may not be entirely to blame for the tragedy. Kian opens up to Jac about his addiction, but remains unsure that she will stand by him now she knows the truth. Alex and Donna kiss, but Donna still finds herself struggling to put Xavier's memory to rest

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Andrew Treagust needed a change of pace and thought this lay in New Zealand, where he could follow his dream of becoming a snowboard instructor. Partner Emma was less than convinced, having a job she loved in the UK and two young boys with close ties to their grandparents. Six years after the family sampled a week 'down under', the programme catches up with them to see how their lives have changed. Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: After an early frost decimated a big part of their crop, father-and-son team Billy and Billy Junior are excited to sell a fair chunk of their organic fruit and nuts at Birmingham's Moseley Farmers' Market. Meanwhile, farmer Leon is hoping his brother-in-law Peter will pull in the punters to try and then buy some of their home-grown, home-made wines and gins. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a factory in rural Somerset that produces one million pots of yoghurt every 24 hours, and meets the herd of cows that produce the milk. Cherry Healey helps out with the UK's biggest blackcurrant harvest and tries out plant-based alternatives to milk, while historian Ruth Goodman reveals the stories behind the electric milk float and the cream tea

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: BBC Bitesize Daily
programme.desc: Nihal Arthanayake presents Motivational Monday with Maths, English and History

programme.title: Magic Numbers: Hannah Fry's Mysterious World of Maths
programme.desc: Mathematician Dr Hannah Fry explores the mystery of maths. In the opening episode, Hannah begins her journey looking at whether maths was invented or discovered, and examines rules of maths found in nature from flower petals to a virus structure, as well as the invention of zero and other imaginary numbers

programme.title: Live International Bowls
programme.desc: Coverage of the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the pairs final and Robert Paxton v Jack Bird in the first round of the open singles event

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Raj Bisram and Adam Partridge assess antiques at Rochester Cathedral in Kent, while presenter Paul Martin explores the story of the French Huguenots

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Maisie Adam, Rory Bremner, James Cracknell and Michelle Gayle go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the post-war British modern art movement in the seaside town of St Ives, the home for leading artists such as Barbara Hepworth and Terry Frost. At the Lizard Peninsula, Rick heads out to sea to fish for red mullet, and on the wild coast at Land's End, he discovers why Cornwall has a rather unusual animal as its national emblem

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: It's the first full week of the challenge and Tom is back in Gloucester, the city where he grew up, to exercise and cook alongside his diet and fitness gang. As team chef, he wants to get them off to a good start on calorie counting - and familiar, comforting dishes is the way to go. Window cleaning brothers-in-law Dan and Alex volunteered for the challenge because of health concerns and a normal day sees them tucking into a big, calorific breakfast and grabbing fast food between jobs, but Tom joins the boys on a bike ride in the local countryside before giving them a tasty breakfast muffin to try

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, asking them questions on the specialist subjects of the London 2012 Olympic Games, JS Haldane, Hancock's Half Hour, and John Wyndham's novels, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another second-round match as the Bridges and the Puzzle Hunters compete for a place in the quarter-finals. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the second of the quarter-finals, two teams of four students compete to make it to the next stage of the contest as they try to win two matches to progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: The return of the documentary about the county's fishing communities, beginning in the popular tourist resort of Newquay as restrictions begin to ease after the coronavirus lockdown. It is not a moment too soon for the town's fishermen, most of whom have been tied up for months, and with lost time to catch up on, and livelihoods on the line, the fleet is steaming into a summer like no other

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz from October 2019, with guests Ross Noble and Rachel Johnson joining regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis and Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Yemen: Coronavirus in a War Zone
programme.desc: BBC Arabic's Nawal al-Maghafi investigates how the humanitarian crisis of the Yemeni civil war has been made worse by the pandemic, revealing the authorities' denial and cover-up

programme.title: International Bowls
programme.desc: Action from today's play at Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, including the open pairs final

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: After the turmoil of 2020 and as a new year gets underway, the team looks at how people can plan to make the countryside a bigger part of their lives? Matt Baker wants to get fitter and tries his hand at cani-cross, Anita Rani straps on her walking boots, Margherita Taylor heads for the woods and Helen Skelton aims to buy local. Tom Heap discovers the joys of 'blue' therapy and Adam Henson gets creative with clay

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The programme returns with Fiona Bruce and the team visiting Forty Hall in Enfield, north London, where items brought in for assessment include a set of Star Wars toys, and a policeman's helmet worn by John Lennon. There is also a rock crystal Faberge cigarette case complete with a ruby, an early post-box, and a poster for one of Pink Floyd's very first concerts

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a single glove uncovered the identities of a prolific and violent gang, and an image of a distinctive ponytail that solved a high-tech bank heist

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Jeff and daughters Tracy and Karen take their honey to Bowhouse Farmers' Market in Fife, where organic vegetable growers Connie and Tom also try to sell their produce

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Hill farmer Joyce Campbell prepares spring, and does everything she can to make sure her 800 North Country Cheviot ewes are healthy ahead of birthing season. Meanwhile in the Lake District, Matt and Dani Blair are preparing for the first cattle TB test on their new farm, and in Aberdeenshire, spring has come early for new entrant farmers Isla and George at Little Rowater Farm

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: Naturalist David Attenborough explores animal oddities, looking at two different creatures with curiously distinctive evolutionary quirks, linked together by a common theme. He examines how hedgehogs and rhinos have developed protective skins, and looks at animals that rely on other species, from cuckoos planting their eggs in other birds' nests to the Death's-head hawkmoth stealing honey

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: BBC Bitesize Daily
programme.desc: Katie Thistleton presents Study Tips Tuesday with the help of Youtuber Jack Edwards

programme.title: Magic Numbers: Hannah Fry's Mysterious World of Maths
programme.desc: Dr Hannah Fry travels down the fastest zip-wire in the world to learn more about Newton's ideas on gravity and discusses why maths is so good at predicting the world around us

programme.title: Live International Bowls
programme.desc: Coverage of the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the mixed pairs final and a second-round open singles match. Nick Brett and Marion Purcell defeated Greg Harlow and Alison Merrien in last year's mixed pairs showpiece contest

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: From Capesthorne Hall in Cheshire, where Mark Stacey finds a horse ledger that is a runaway success and Caroline Hawley uncovers a set of letters from Sir Arthur Conan Doyle

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Maisie Adam, Rory Bremner, James Cracknell and Michelle Gayle go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the Roseland Peninsula, beginning at a spot believed by some to have been visited by Jesus Christ. He tours the bay of St Mawes with Alex and Olga Polizzi, who own one of Cornwall's most luxurious hotels, and meets one of the county's oldest butchers, who demonstrates the ancient practice of aging meat. Finally, in the harbourside village of Mousehole, Rick meets a potter who finds inspiration from the local landscape

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: A family of five works in a corner shop to reveal how such businesses have changed over the course of the past 100 years, aided by presenter Sara Cox and social historian Polly Russell. The first episode covers shops from the Victorian era to the First World War. The family must learn to bake their own counter goods, measure loose products like tea, flour and sugar by hand, and make deliveries with a horse and cart. They also receive a visit from Kelly Holmes, who puts them through an Edwardian workout and discover the impact of food shortages on corner shops as the war drew on

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Winter wildlife stories from around the UK. Chris Packham will be in the New Forest watching winter transform his home, and Iolo Williams is at the Centre for Alternative Technology in Wales, hoping to see some murmurations. Young naturalist Indy Green will be taking us deep into Sherwood Forest on the hunt for one of our most elusive birds of prey - the goshawk

programme.title: Silenced: The Hidden Story of Disabled Britain
programme.desc: Cerrie Burnell presents a history of disabled people's struggle for human rights in Britain. The film looks back to the policies in place in Victorian workhouses, uncovering the original records of an institution created to segregate disabled people and stop them from having children. She also shares inspiring stories of pioneering campaigners for social change, and looks at the challenges still to be faced in the future

programme.title: Elis James: Funny Nation
programme.desc: The stand-up explores the influence of Wales' different performance traditions on the nation's comedy and on its biggest stars. He discovers the lasting legacy of the popular 70s double act Ryan and Ronnie on some of today's biggest performers, finds out how the folk club scene helped produce Max Boyce and sees how Rhod Gilbert has inspired a new generation of performers - including the latest winner of the Edinburgh comedy award Jordan Brookes

programme.title: Comedians: Home Alone
programme.desc: Tim Key performs his isolation poetry, Morgana Robinson showcases her impersonation of Cheryl Cole and Charlie Higson brings The Fast Show's Swiss Toni back to life. Plus, Bob Mortimer shows what it is possible to create from a slab of butter, a tin of sardines and an orange

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Last Woman on Earth with Sara Pascoe
programme.desc: The comedian's world tour of unusual jobs ends in Finland, where she discovers that climate change has had a serious impact on many traditional trades. In the south of the country, she explores the timber trade by meeting a forest cleaner and a baker. Venturing north, where temperatures are rising the most, she gets to grips with the tourist industry by training as an ice carver and an elf in Santa's village

programme.title: International Bowls
programme.desc: Action from today's play at Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, including the mixed pairs final

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Part one of two. Monty Don and his team of horticultural experts feature in this selection of highlights from the 2020 series

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A plastic bag heat sealer that convicted an online drug trafficker. Plus, a casual chat with a criminal and a map join the dots to catch three burglars terrorising a neighbourhood

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Victorian Slum
programme.desc: The volunteers experience the living and working conditions of the 1870s, which was blighted by a dire economic depression. The Potters can no longer rely on breadwinner Graham as he struggles to find work so they join forces with single parent Shazeda to try to survive by making artificial flowers, while there are new arrivals from Ireland in the form of siblings John and Maria Barker, who are horrified by the conditions that would have greeted Irish migrants to Victorian London

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon begins the last leg of his journey in Nicaragua and visits the Rama-Kriol people, who face losing their ancestral homes due to the construction of a new transoceanic waterway. Simon then discovers Hondurans living in the grip of some of the most violent criminal gangs in the world, with San Pedro Sula having the highest murder rate of any city on the planet. His journey ends in Jamaica, where he spends time with young people who have rejected gang life, offering a model of hope for future generations

programme.title: Ireland to Sydney by Any Means
programme.desc: Series following Charley Boorman through 25 countries, as he tries to travel by land and sea from his home in Co Wicklow to Australia on more than 100 local forms of transport. After deciding on a route that avoids the most dangerous places on Earth, he, his producer and his cameraman begin the first leg of the journey across the Irish Sea to England

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Maggie Aderin-Pocock and Chris Lintott look back at some of the biggest stories of 2020, from the launch of Solar Orbiter to the discovery of phosphine on Venus

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: The story of how the solar system will be transformed by the ageing Sun, before coming to an end in approximately eight billion years. Astronomers can predict how it will happen by analysing distant galaxies, stars and planets in their final moments, and the programme brings these predictions to life with a giant scale model of the solar system spread out all over a town in the American Midwest

programme.title: Light and Dark
programme.desc: Part two of two. Professor Jim Al-Khalili visits the Gran Sasso National Laboratory in Italy, where scientists are attempting to capture and study dark matter. He also reveals how the discovery that the expansion of the universe is accelerating has led to theories that a mysterious new force called dark energy is in the process of gradually tearing the universe apart

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon begins the last leg of his journey in Nicaragua and visits the Rama-Kriol people, who face losing their ancestral homes due to the construction of a new transoceanic waterway. Simon then discovers Hondurans living in the grip of some of the most violent criminal gangs in the world, with San Pedro Sula having the highest murder rate of any city on the planet. His journey ends in Jamaica, where he spends time with young people who have rejected gang life, offering a model of hope for future generations

programme.title: The Victorian Slum

programme.title: Ireland to Sydney by Any Means
programme.desc: Series following Charley Boorman through 25 countries, as he tries to travel by land and sea from his home in Co Wicklow to Australia on more than 100 local forms of transport. After deciding on a route that avoids the most dangerous places on Earth, he, his producer and his cameraman begin the first leg of the journey across the Irish Sea to England

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain
programme.desc: Fred takes his traction engine to Scotland, where it becomes the first vehicle of its kind to cross the Forth Road Bridge under its own steam. He also visits the Falkirk Wheel and one of the region's few surviving traditional ironworks

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an oval picture featuring a cabin surrounded by fresh snowfall and colourful skies

programme.title: Secrets of the Museum
programme.desc: Project manager Alice and her team unframe and scan a gallery of 500-year-old masterpieces - Raphael's cartoons, removing frames that have sealed them in for 30 years. While in the V&A's stunning jewellery galleries, newly-appointed keeper James must check some of the most fragile objects in the collection - the jewellery made with moving parts. Among the treasures are a spectacular butterfly ring worn by Beyoncé that appears to take flight with each gentle flex of the finger

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic examines techniques and conventions within a genre that mixes film and music and encompasses many forms, from drama and comedy to fantasy and documentary. There is the classic pop star vehicle, where the biggest acts play themselves, or a version of themselves, as well as dramatised accounts of the stars' lives that dazzle with musical set pieces, while playing on the fascination with the darker side of fame

programme.title: A Hard Day's Night (1964)
programme.desc: The Beatles, harassed by their manager and Paul's grandfather, embark on a train journey to London for a TV show, but find themselves dogged by all manner of problems, including the inevitable overexcited fans. Musical comedy, starring the Fab Four alongside Wilfrid Brambell, Victor Spinetti and Anna Quayle. Songs include Can't Buy Me Love, Don't Bother Me and the title track

programme.title: The World's Most Photographed
programme.desc: An exploration of how the camera transformed the relationship between the monarchy and the British people during the 64-year reign of Queen Victoria, whose accession to the throne in 1837 coincided with the invention of photography. The monarch, whose private life became more visible than ever before, managed to turn the pursuit from a pastime into a political weapon in her battle to safeguard the British throne

programme.title: Victorian Sensations
programme.desc: Actor Paul McGann explores the 1890s, a decade when science, entertainment, art and morality collided. He discovers how the works of HG Wells, Aubrey Beardsley and Oscar Wilde were shaped by fears of moral, social and racial degeneration, and explains how Wilde sought to subvert traditional Victorian values

programme.title: Handmade in Mexico
programme.desc: Following the process of the creation of a clay sculpture known as the Tree of Life, which was originally intended to teach bible stories to the indigenous people of Puebla

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an oval picture featuring a cabin surrounded by fresh snowfall and colourful skies

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain
programme.desc: Fred takes his traction engine to Scotland, where it becomes the first vehicle of its kind to cross the Forth Road Bridge under its own steam. He also visits the Falkirk Wheel and one of the region's few surviving traditional ironworks

programme.title: Secrets of the Museum

Among the treasures are a spectacular butterfly ring worn by Beyoncé that appears to take flight with each gentle flex of the finger

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain
programme.desc: The former steeplejack's journey takes him to the North East, where he visits a pumping station that ran for 100 years before ceasing operation in 1967 and travels across the Middlesbrough transporter bridge. He also discovers a company in Bradford which still makes boilers in the old-fashioned way, with rivets instead of welding

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: The American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a cold, snow-blanketed wintertime landscape scene

programme.title: Inside Obama's White House
programme.desc: Examining how Barack Obama set out to end George Bush's wars in the Middle East and reset relations with the rest of the world. In Syria, when evidence shows the use of chemical weapons, Obama decides to bomb. But when the British Parliament votes against the intervention, he decides he needs the backing of a reluctant Congress. Foreign Secretary William Hague explains why the British Parliament voted against intervention in 2013. This episode also explores how Obama scored a big win when he negotiated a secret deal to end the nuclear threat from Iran - behind the backs of his closest allies

programme.title: 'Til Kingdom Come: Trump, Faith and Money
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the close ties between US evangelicals and Israel's foremost philanthropic organisation, and its influence on both nations' foreign policies

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Journalist and author Jonathan Freedland examines the US presidency and explores what qualities make a great American leader. He is joined by contributors including James Naughtie, Shirley Williams, Harold Evans, Douglas Hurd, Simon Hoggart and Bonnie Greer to discuss what can be learnt from presidents such as Theodore Roosevelt, John F Kennedy, Richard Nixon and Jimmy Carter

programme.title: Barack Obama Talks to David Olusoga
programme.desc: Historian David Olusoga is joined in conversation by former American President Barack Obama to discuss his long-awaited memoir, A Promised Land. He also reflects on the volatile racial divide in the US, his steadfast refusal to abandon American ideals, and revealing details of life as a black First Family in the White House

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Josephine has been blackmailed by the traffickers into defending their smuggler, while Ali and Laure follow whatever leads they can tease out of the new line of enquiry, but how much information can they get out of their new arrest? Captain Beckriche and judge Lucie Bordieu must find common ground to prove a link between the international smuggling operation and the murder case. In French

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Laure and Ali identify 'the Alsatian' and get to work on a tracking device that was hidden under his car by members of a rival gang. Josephine tries to help Souleymane, but her attempts are inadequate. In French

programme.title: Maddie, Space and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie as she finds out how space rockets work and how they take astronauts into space. We discover how thrust and rocket fuel work, when a rocket engine blasts off from the launch pad and into space, and learn about the different parts of the rocket and what they are for. Next, we find out about the special computers on-board that guide astronauts through space, and Maddie rides a very fast rollercoaster to show us how G-force works when a rocket launches. Finally, we find out how a rocket lands safely to bring the astronauts back to Earth.

programme.title: CBeebies Stargazing
programme.desc: Inspired by the Voyager missions, CBeebies Stargazing: Space Explorers takes pre-schoolers on a journey of discovery through the Solar System. Before embarking on the voyage, Maggie tells the story of two small spacecrafts Voyager 1 and Voyager 2 and introduces everyone to Space Sailor, the canal boat that will take her and Chris to visit the giant planets. Speaking from Mission Control, Robert the Robot instructs Chris, Maggie and the Stargazers to set a course for the Asteroid Belt. Soon Robert receives an incoming message from Astronomer Edward, who uploads some telescope footage of the Moon for Stargazers everywhere to take a close up look at Earth’s nearest neighbour. Back on Space Sailor, Chris discovers it’s going to take a while longer to get to the Asteroid Belt, and Maggie explains how it’s much further away than the Moon. programme.desc: Mister Maker uses his trusty Makerphone to call one of his famous friends - the incredible Jay Blades! Mister M asks Jay for help when trying to repair an old egg box. The friends decide that a Treasure Trunk needs to be made, and this proves to be an exciting and very useful idea. Mister Maker, knowing that Jay is famous for his splashes of bright colour within his designs, uses the same approach too. Later, Mister Maker checks in on his friends, The Shapes. They are snoring away, so Mister Maker makes sure he leaves them to have a nice rest. Mister Maker then decides to dance like a Shape himself, which in turn inspires him to make some shape art. This leads to another arty idea where some paper plates are put to good use. Lastly, Mister Maker remembers all the fun he has had throughout the day.

programme.title: CBeebies Stargazing
programme.desc: Inspired by the Voyager missions, CBeebies Stargazing: Space Explorers takes pre-schoolers on a journey of discovery through the Solar System. Speaking from Mission Control, Robert the Robot instructs Chris, Maggie and the Stargazers to set a course for the giant planet, Jupiter. Excited for the next stage of their journey, Chris notices that the plant pots on top of the Space Sailor are a model of the Solar System and realises that there is a lot of space between the asteroid belt and Jupiter. Maggie explains that Jupiter is too far away for humans to visit so spacecrafts like Voyager make the journey for us. Robert receives an incoming message from Astronomer Edward, who uploads some mission data of Jupiter for Stargazers everywhere to find out how many of Jupiter’s moons they can spot. Back on the Space Sailor, Chris and Maggie encounter their first canal lock and discover that it’s going to take a while longer to get to Jupiter. More Stargazers send an update back to Mission Contro

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: When JoJo can't find her favourite Captain Chloe storybook, Gran Gran suggests they make up their own story. As they create their pirate quest, JoJo learns that when you make up your own story, you get to decide what happens next.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck

programme.title: Clangers

programme.title: Breakfast
