programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Leicester City v Southampton at King Power Stadium and Wolverhampton Wanderers v West Bromwich Albion at Molineux. The Saints beat Leicester 2-1 here last term to gain a measure of revenge for a stunning 9-0 hammering in the reverse fixture. The first Black Country derby took place today since the Baggies prevailed 5-1 here in the 2011/12 season. Plus, Leeds United v Brighton & Hove Albion, West Ham United v Burnley and Aston Villa v Everton

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew's guests are Sir Simon Stevens, chief executive of NHS England, Dominic Raab MP, foreign secretary, Ed Davey MP, leader of the Liberal Democrats, Gloria Borger, CNN chief political analyst. News review with Hugh Pym, BBC health editor and Lucy Fisher, deputy political editor, Daily Telegraph

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: All the latest political news from Wales and beyond, presented by James Williams

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell returns with the programme that features moral and religious discussions on topical issues

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Kiwi-born Felicity stayed in the UK to raise her daughter, but now wants to relocate to Australia. However, daughter Lucy says she doesn't want to go, so the pair sample a week 'Down Under'. At the end of the trial, Lucy has to vote on where her future lies

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant help two teams at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, Powys, while Charlie Ross finds out about a collection of vintage film posters

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Singer and new presenter Brenda Edwards visits an outdoor walking church in east London, while Katie Piper meets a vicar who has become a hit on social media with 60-second sermons

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple hoping to relocate to rural Somerset on a budget of £700,000, looking for a home with enough land for animals and a glamping site. Away from the hunt, Alistair gets stuck in with some Cheddar making in the county where it originated

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore saves three things about to be dumped at a tip, transforms them into bespoke and valuable pieces, before selling them and giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of them in the first place

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to West Bromwich, where Pauline recently bought a house complete with her very first garden. However, she has no idea what to do with the plot, which is currently full of weeds and random bits of old broken junk

programme.title: Carol Vorderman: Closer to Home
programme.desc: Carol revisits her schooldays in Rhyl and meets some of the region's inspirational teachers, including a woman who has been teaching children karate since the age of 12. She also has a go at harness-racing, joins a school science lesson involving a zip line, and is reunited with her step-family for a birthday celebration

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen takes a sideways look at Alternative Wales, featuring clips of belly dancers, hamster lovers and pylon enthusiast

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Tom Heap head to Somerset to visit the Mendips to find out how this stunning but fragile area is being protected from the invasion of visitors. Ellie scales Cheddar Gorge as well as discovering the damage cars are wreaking on narrow winding roads, and how white-clawed freshwater crayfish are being saved from voracious invaders. Tom finds out how the countryside can help transform lives and also investigates if the government's new green subsidy scheme can really deliver for farmers. Adam Henson meets ex-international rugby union ref turned Nigel Owens, who is now a farmer and facing his herd's first TB test

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Christchurch Park in Ipswich, where items assessed include an heirloom Rolex, memorabilia related to Donald Campbell's land-speed record and a necklace worth half a million pounds. There is also a rare Roman relic dug up in the garden and a pocket watch with a hidden secret

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at how the uneven distribution of freshwater around the globe has given rise to an incredible diversity of species and habits, from the driest desert to the lushest tropical rainforest. Featuring a colony of ants banding together into a raft every time its home in the Amazon floods, a rain frog that manages to eke out an existence in one of the world's driest habitats on Earth, and the last wild camels that survive the Gobi Desert's bitter winters by eating snow that blows in from Siberia

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Charles, Marie-Andree and Ajay flee Bangkok for Nepal, where they scour the streets for victims, but tensions arise when Charles loses the group's money in a card game until Marie-Andree overcomes her crisis of conscience to deliver their next prey. In March 1976, Nadine helps Herman and Angela to accumulate evidence by taking undercover photos of the suspects and their private apartment

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Liverpool v Manchester United, Sheffield United v Tottenham Hotspur and Manchester City v Crystal Palace. The Reds won last season's corresponding fixture 2-0 en route to winning the title and another such result would further cement their status as title-favourites. Meanwhile, Spurs and City will have expected victories to boost their own chances of success. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Kelly Somers presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Manchester United, Manchester City v Aston Villa and Reading v Arsenal

programme.title: All the President's Men (1976)
programme.desc: The true story of Washington Post reporters Bob Woodward and Carl Bernstein, who persevered in their investigation of the links between the Watergate break-in and the White House, a scandal that eventually forced the resignation of President Nixon. Alan J Pakula's drama, starring Robert Redford, Dustin Hoffman, Jason Robards and Hal Holbrook

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live

programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell check out a flat in Southfields, south-west London, a maisonette in Wolverhampton and a three-floor terrace house in Ashton-under-Lyne in Greater Manchester

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare indulgent treats for when family and friends come on over - a baked cheesecake, a crowd-pleasing terrine and the ultimate beef sandwich

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown helps a couple from Buckinghamshire who are looking for a detached property in Rutland with a garden big enough for a vegetable patch, all on their budget of £500,000. Continuing the horticultural theme, Steve visits Barnsdale Gardens to see for himself the legacy left by Geoff Hamilton, one of the country's best-loved gardeners

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Jeff and daughters Tracy and Karen take their honey to Bowhouse Farmers' Market in Fife, where organic vegetable growers Connie and Tom also try to sell their produce

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against one another to buy a collection of curiosities, an unusual chair, a mask, candlesticks from a famous maker, and a writing slope. Nigel Havers presents

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones hosts first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Mark Daly investigates why black men in the UK are more likely than white men to have force used on them by police, and to die in police custody. He follows the family of Kevin Clarke on their search for justice after Mr Clarke repeatedly said 'I can't breathe' as he was restrained by police on the ground for 14 minutes during a mental health crisis, and died soon afterwards. The programme also reveals fresh evidence in Scotland's most high-profile death in custody after Sheku Bayoh died in 2015 after being restrained by up to six officers

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda suggests going out for Mick's birthday, but he is more concerned with Tina as she never usually forgets his big day. Frankie pushes Stuart for information on Mick, while Jay is shocked to learn Rainie has been earning extra cash on the side doing house clearances for customers. Max continues to pressure Ian to take a test, and Sharon feels guilty when Kathy tells her she knows her son is unwell

programme.title: Nightshifters
programme.desc: The programme follows Kevin, who manages a 10-strong team recycling clothing hangers for the UK's largest department stores, and Polish-born Marek, who works on his own milking 100 dairy cows. Two women have dreams of escaping the night shift - Christiana would love to open a coffee shop and Tammy an animal rescue centre

programme.title: Traces
programme.desc: Emma's accusation against Phil drives a wedge between her and Daniel, while McKinven urges her to stop her own amateur investigation as he becomes certain that the person who killed her mother is known to her

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: David and Michael are butting heads, a situation not helped by having to read with various new potential cast members. They are increasingly annoyed at each new interaction, while Simon tries to keep the ship steady and Georgia marshals her charity team

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The return of the competition in which contestants fight to be crowned the next superstar drag queen, with guest judge Elizabeth Hurley joining Michelle Visage and Graham Norton. In the opening edition, the hopefuls take on their first challenge in the form of a photoshoot with RuPaul, and then are asked to serve two looks on the runway

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Bodyguard and Durrells star Keeley Hawes joins the host to discuss her new drama series Finding Alice, while writer and one-time Doctor Who co-star Noel Clarke talks about his cop drama Bulletproof: South Africa. Hollywood star Rebel Wilson chats about Straight Talking, which sees her collaborate with former soldier Ant Middleton, and eminent film-maker M Night Shyamalan promotes his new psychological horror series Servant. Comedian Daniel Sloss also guests and Jake Bugg performs his current single All I Need

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: With millions of people swapping the gym for the living room during the Covid-19 pandemic, Mehreen Baig delves into the latest science to find the best way to get fit at home. She uncovers exciting new research testing the theory that just one minute of strength training per week on each main muscle group might reduce your risk of diabetes and heart disease, and also looks at the impact wearable fitness tech actually has on our willpower to stick to an exercise plan. Later, she investigates whether popular supplements like pre-trainers and protein shakes really work

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Angela Rippon and Gloria Hunniford investigate issues affecting viewers in lockdown, including poor delivery service from one of the biggest names in the business

programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with the Hagos family two years after they gave life in New Zealand a trial week, and discovers if concerns over their youngest son's welfare were founded

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a con woman's fake claims of having cancer were exposed by her distinctive wallpaper, and the hidden compartment in a car that brought a gang defrauding charities to justice

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Raj Bisram help two teams look for items at Bath Racecourse, while presenter Eric Knowles look at Chinese ceramics with an eye-watering price tag

programme.title: Father Brown

programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers create a twist on a classic pudding, rustle up a teatime treat, and use two breakfast favourites to make a new dish

programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a family from Berkshire to find a new home in Devon where the children can enjoy the great outdoors, and visits a farm that grows edible flowers

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Mike and grandson Jan prepare for Petersfield market, hoping to sell their cheeses and home-grown veg alongside Phil and Sue, selling their 100-year-old secret-recipe sausages

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents the show in which five dealers bid to buy a retro coat stand, a train set, an unusual chair, some maritime history and a quirky house-clearance find

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A hungover Frankie finds Mick, and despite the tension she wishes him a happy birthday, before revealing she is leaving today. Will he stop her getting on a plane to Australia or let her go? With her conscience is pricking at her, Sharon tells Phil she cannot go through with their plan - so he reassures her he will get something stronger to do the job much more quickly. Iqra has exciting news for her family

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Skylar struggles to forgive herself as the hearing reaches a verdict. Jodie's campaign against Beka puts Sacha's life in danger too, and the only hope is that Lucky can work out what is going on before it's too late. While Jac tries to throw herself into her working day, she is unable to shake her concerns about what Kian is getting up to

programme.title: Carol Vorderman: Closer to Home
programme.desc: Carol Vorderman explores how technology and engineering are shaping the future of North Wales on the last leg of her journey, from Anglesey to Snowdonia. She puts her piloting skills to the test in an RAF flight simulator, meets a paralympic athlete who has started a business manufacturing ife-changing prosthetic leg covers, and takes an eco-friendly trip to the peak of Snoadown in an electric hybrid train

programme.title: Traces
programme.desc: While Kathy oversees the excavation of Marie's grave, Daniel gives McKinven the lead he needs to make an arrest, but it's the exacting forensic science that finally brings peace for Emma

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: Simon receives desperate news from Georgia, and makes yet another attempt to get David and Michael to work together. However, it ultimately falls to a final pair of new potential cast members to offer the pair a new perspective on their friendship before it is too late

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Bethan Rhys Roberts presents a weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters

programme.title: BAMOUS
programme.desc: Dane Baptiste hosts a topical comedy show exploring the NASBLAQ - a mythical stock index for black performers - through stand-up routines and sketches. Featuring appearances by a exciting up-and-coming comedians

programme.title: Supercar, Superfam
programme.desc: One-off special catching up with Kash and Shabs in the wake of a disastrous fire, as they make plans to restore the custom supercar business bigger and better than before. programme.desc: The Rev Jane Denniston of Campsie Parish Church and Fr John Deighan of St Machan's in Lennoxtown present from the foyer of BBC Glasgow, with live music by Rachel Hair and Ron Jappy

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Dave and Harry Rich head to West Sussex, where they come up with ideas for the owners of a narrow garden who want to modernise their space

programme.title: The Crash Detectives
programme.desc: A Friday evening trip out in the car ends in a fatal crash, but the driver refuses to accept his driving is to blame. The collision team must find the evidence to prove the real story of the night

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Singer and new presenter Brenda Edwards visits an outdoor walking church in east London, while Katie Piper meets a vicar who has become a hit on social media with 60-second sermons

programme.title: Escape to the Country

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year
programme.desc: The judges have scoured the country, visiting 27 incredible homes and now, the finalists gather as the judges choose the overall winner

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: Examining the solar force that powers Earth and how plants and animals have adapted to the uneven amounts of sunlight given to different parts of the planet over the course of a year. The exception is on the equator, where the duration of day and night remain the same throughout the year. Here, this guaranteed sunlight drives the great diversity of life in the tropical jungles

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Tom Heap head to Somerset to visit the Mendips to find out how this stunning but fragile area is being protected from the invasion of visitors. Ellie scales Cheddar Gorge as well as discovering the damage cars are wreaking on narrow winding roads, and how white-clawed freshwater crayfish are being saved from voracious invaders. Tom finds out how the countryside can help transform lives and also investigates if the government's new green subsidy scheme can really deliver for farmers. Adam Henson meets ex-international rugby union ref turned Nigel Owens, who is now a farmer and facing his herd's first TB test

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Christchurch Park in Ipswich, where items assessed include an heirloom Rolex, memorabilia related to Donald Campbell's land-speed record and a necklace worth half a million pounds. There is also a rare Roman relic dug up in the garden and a pocket watch with a hidden secret

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at how the uneven distribution of freshwater around the globe has given rise to an incredible diversity of species and habits, from the driest desert to the lushest tropical rainforest. Featuring a colony of ants banding together into a raft every time its home in the Amazon floods, a rain frog that manages to eke out an existence in one of the world's driest habitats on Earth, and the last wild camels that survive the Gobi Desert's bitter winters by eating snow that blows in from Siberia

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Charles, Marie-Andree and Ajay flee Bangkok for Nepal, where they scour the streets for victims, but tensions arise when Charles loses the group's money in a card game until Marie-Andree overcomes her crisis of conscience to deliver their next prey. In March 1976, Nadine helps Herman and Angela to accumulate evidence by taking undercover photos of the suspects and their private apartment

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Liverpool v Manchester United, Sheffield United v Tottenham Hotspur and Manchester City v Crystal Palace. The Reds won last season's corresponding fixture 2-0 en route to winning the title and another such result would further cement their status as title-favourites. Meanwhile, Spurs and City will have expected victories to boost their own chances of success. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Motherwell v Rangers. Steven Thompson presents action from the Premiership clash at Fir Park. Plus, a round-up of Saturday's goals

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live

They begin with reports on the scammers capitalising on the pandemic, including criminals targeting those waiting for vaccines

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Host Eric Knowles enters the world of travel and transport, as he takes a look back at some of the most interesting past purchases in that genre. programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare indulgent treats for when family and friends come on over - a baked cheesecake, a crowd-pleasing terrine and the ultimate beef sandwich

programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with a couple who sampled a week in Melbourne, when Nat Baker hoped to sway his sceptical wife Gemma into making the move to Australia

programme.title: Coronavirus: Scotland's Response
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Scottish Government's briefing, with reaction, expert analysis and the very latest updates from Scotland and beyond on the pandemic.

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A mysterious saboteur tries to stop Kembleford winning the Three Counties choir competition. Return of the 1950s-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams as the Roman Catholic priest who attempts to solve various misdemeanours

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A distinctive sticker on a laptop finally links a man to a string of burglaries that had terrified a community, and shiny copper hair dye puts a dangerous armed robber behind bars

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown helps a couple from Buckinghamshire who are looking for a detached property in Rutland with a garden big enough for a vegetable patch, all on their budget of £500,000. Continuing the horticultural theme, Steve visits Barnsdale Gardens to see for himself the legacy left by Geoff Hamilton, one of the country's best-loved gardeners

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Jeff and daughters Tracy and Karen take their honey to Bowhouse Farmers' Market in Fife, where organic vegetable growers Connie and Tom also try to sell their produce

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against one another to buy a collection of curiosities, an unusual chair, a mask, candlesticks from a famous maker, and a writing slope. Nigel Havers presents

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones hosts first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Mark Daly investigates why black men in the UK are more likely than white men to have force used on them by police, and to die in police custody. He follows the family of Kevin Clarke on their search for justice after Mr Clarke repeatedly said 'I can't breathe' as he was restrained by police on the ground for 14 minutes during a mental health crisis, and died soon afterwards. The programme also reveals fresh evidence in Scotland's most high-profile death in custody after Sheku Bayoh died in 2015 after being restrained by up to six officers

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda suggests going out for Mick's birthday, but he is more concerned with Tina as she never usually forgets his big day. Frankie pushes Stuart for information on Mick, while Jay is shocked to learn Rainie has been earning extra cash on the side doing house clearances for customers. Max continues to pressure Ian to take a test, and Sharon feels guilty when Kathy tells her she knows her son is unwell

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben and Susan hold their breath as they wait to see if they are expecting a baby, fearful they could produce another one just like Nick. Ben's new assistant Sally puts all his patients off by reacting queasily to the blood that is part and parcel of the job

programme.title: Traces
programme.desc: Emma's accusation against Phil drives a wedge between her and Daniel, while McKinven urges her to stop her own amateur investigation as he becomes certain that the person who killed her mother is known to her

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: David and Michael are butting heads, a situation not helped by having to read with various new potential cast members. They are increasingly annoyed at each new interaction, while Simon tries to keep the ship steady and Georgia marshals her charity team

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The return of the competition in which contestants fight to be crowned the next superstar drag queen, with guest judge Elizabeth Hurley joining Michelle Visage and Graham Norton. In the opening edition, the hopefuls take on their first challenge in the form of a photoshoot with RuPaul, and then are asked to serve two looks on the runway

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Bodyguard and Durrells star Keeley Hawes joins the host to discuss her new drama series Finding Alice, while writer and one-time Doctor Who co-star Noel Clarke talks about his cop drama Bulletproof: South Africa. Hollywood star Rebel Wilson chats about Straight Talking, which sees her collaborate with former soldier Ant Middleton, and eminent film-maker M Night Shyamalan promotes his new psychological horror series Servant. Comedian Daniel Sloss also guests and Jake Bugg performs his current single All I Need

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: With millions of people swapping the gym for the living room during the Covid-19 pandemic, Mehreen Baig delves into the latest science to find the best way to get fit at home. She uncovers exciting new research testing the theory that just one minute of strength training per week on each main muscle group might reduce your risk of diabetes and heart disease, and also looks at the impact wearable fitness tech actually has on our willpower to stick to an exercise plan. Later, she investigates whether popular supplements like pre-trainers and protein shakes really work

programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with the Hagos family two years after they gave life in New Zealand a trial week, and discovers if concerns over their youngest son's welfare were founded

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A hungover Frankie finds Mick, and despite the tension she wishes him a happy birthday, before revealing she is leaving today. Will he stop her getting on a plane to Australia or let her go? With her conscience is pricking at her, Sharon tells Phil she cannot go through with their plan - so he reassures her he will get something stronger to do the job much more quickly. Iqra has exciting news for her family

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Lenny is determined to keep Amber close to home, but his plans are not met with universal approval. Cameron's attempts to alleviate Dylan's anxieties go awry and Ellie's desire for justice only succeeds in angering Nicole

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Skylar struggles to forgive herself as the hearing reaches a verdict. Jodie's campaign against Beka puts Sacha's life in danger too, and the only hope is that Lucky can work out what is going on before it's too late. While Jac tries to throw herself into her working day, she is unable to shake her concerns about what Kian is getting up to

programme.title: Traces
programme.desc: While Kathy oversees the excavation of Marie's grave, Daniel gives McKinven the lead he needs to make an arrest, but it's the exacting forensic science that finally brings peace for Emma

programme.title: Staged

programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a family from Berkshire to find a new home in Devon where the children can enjoy the great outdoors, and visits a farm that grows edible flowers

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Mike and grandson Jan prepare for Petersfield market, hoping to sell their cheeses and home-grown veg alongside Phil and Sue, selling their 100-year-old secret-recipe sausages

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents the show in which five dealers bid to buy a retro coat stand, a train set, an unusual chair, some maritime history and a quirky house-clearance find

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A hungover Frankie finds Mick, and despite the tension she wishes him a happy birthday, before revealing she is leaving today. Will he stop her getting on a plane to Australia or let her go? With her conscience is pricking at her, Sharon tells Phil she cannot go through with their plan - so he reassures her he will get something stronger to do the job much more quickly. Iqra has exciting news for her family

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Skylar struggles to forgive herself as the hearing reaches a verdict. Jodie's campaign against Beka puts Sacha's life in danger too, and the only hope is that Lucky can work out what is going on before it's too late. While Jac tries to throw herself into her working day, she is unable to shake her concerns about what Kian is getting up to

programme.title: The Crash Detectives
programme.desc: When a head-on crash leaves a grandfather dead, Chris discovers the answers could lie in electronic data within the car. As his mission to access it begins, he unearths an intriguing story of what really led up to the incident

programme.title: Traces
programme.desc: While Kathy oversees the excavation of Marie's grave, Daniel gives McKinven the lead he needs to make an arrest, but it's the exacting forensic science that finally brings peace for Emma

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: Simon receives desperate news from Georgia, and makes yet another attempt to get David and Michael to work together. However, it ultimately falls to a final pair of new potential cast members to offer the pair a new perspective on their friendship before it is too late

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Documentary following the fortunes of Irish dancers Amy-Mae Dolan from Co Tyrone, Natasia Petracic from Australia and Ireland's Meadhbh Kennedy as they compete for the female lead role in a special 25th anniversary performance of hit show Riverdance at Dublin's 3Arena

programme.title: BAMOUS
programme.desc: Dane Baptiste hosts a topical comedy show exploring the NASBLAQ - a mythical stock index for black performers - through stand-up routines and sketches. Featuring appearances by a exciting up-and-coming comedians

programme.title: Supercar, Superfam
programme.desc: One-off special catching up with Kash and Shabs in the wake of a disastrous fire, as they make plans to restore the custom supercar business bigger and better than before. Kash is unaware that Sheila is making big plans for their wedding anniversary, and a new client presents the team with a challenge that stretches their skills to the limit

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the cash prize

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Captain Matt Dawson is joined by British athletics stars Adam Gemili and Eilidh Doyle, while Phil Tufnell teams up with former footballers Leon Osman and Stephen Warnock. Sue Barker presents

programme.desc: House of Commons proceedings in Westminster Hall from Wednesday 13 January

programme.desc: Questions in the House of Commons to the Women and Equalities Minister Liz Truss and her team

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Donald Trump has secured his unique place in the history books, the first President in American history to be impeached twice. What that means in practical terms isn't clear. There'll be no Trump trial in the senate before Joe Biden moves into the White House but Democrats insist he will be held to account for the assault on the Capitol. Stephen Sackur speaks to the veteran lawyer Alan Dershowitz, part of the Trump defence team in impeachment 1. Will he get involved in the sequel and how will it play in a divided America?

programme.desc: Meanbhag humiliates Mùmantrobha in front of Beadag and he tries to get rid of her – but he regrets this afterwards

programme.title: Port Pàipeir
programme.desc: As Matilda and her friends enter the forest where they are camping, Matilda touches a tree and it comes to life. Thanks to her power, trees can talk and Matilda learns their secret

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including a foolproof way to detect whether friends are telling lies and how to make a mud-brick mansion

programme.title: Dealbhan Fraoich (Heather's Portraits)
programme.desc: While on Skye, Heather Dewar draws a charcoal portrait of Donald 'The Hen' MacDonald

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: A focus on the relationship between film director Alfred Hitchcock and actress Ingrid Bergman

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Archive footage of active children playing street football and walking

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Gàrradh Phàdruig
programme.desc: Spring has arrived and Peter is finally planting out in the garden, while Coinneach helps to paint the walls and the greenhouse starts to fill up with seedlings

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: A portrait of the Pakistani community on Lewis and Harris, whose story goes back 60 years, with 2018 seeing them establish their own mosque in Stornoway

programme.title: Cuirm @ Celtic
programme.desc: An in-concert special from indie-folk act The Staves, recorded at Kelvingrove Museum and Art Gallery for Celtic Connections 2021

programme.title: Sulaisgeir
programme.desc: Once a year a small group of men from Ness on Lewis makes a 40-mile sea journey north to catch a special seabird - the guga. The hunt has barely changed in centuries, and this documentary explores this unique tradition through interviews with those who have taken part over the years, alongside rarely seen archive

programme.title: TradFest
programme.desc: Allan MacDonald presents music from TradFest Music Festival, 2018. Amongst the performers are Frankie Gavin and Fionnán Mac Gabhann

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Ceitidh Morag (Katie Morag)
programme.desc: Neilly Beag goes in search of a new challenge now that the ferry is in operation, so Ceitidh offers her some advice

programme.title: Pincidh Dincidh Dù (Pinky Dinky Doo)
programme.desc: A new superhero visits Baile Mor, so Pinky tries to report on the story

programme.title: Shane an Chef
programme.desc: Bea is very unsettled when Penny leaves her with Shane and Izzy

programme.title: Abadas
programme.desc: The Abadas are busy as they hunt for Hari's toy duck Quack and today's word

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Pàdruig Morrison reads Cù-iasg, written by Gillian Shields, illustrated by Dan Taylor and translated into Gaelic by Jo Macdonald

programme.title: Ronia, Nighean a' Mheirlich/Ronja, the Robber's Daughter
programme.desc: Ronia meets Biorc, the son of her father’s greatest adversary, at Dìg an Dealanaich

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: It's party time and the competitors are full of fun and joyfulness, but it's unlikely that the neighbours will approve of the noise

programme.title: An Rud As Fheàrr Leam
programme.desc: Lachlan from Ross-shire talks about his favourite hobbies, which include karate, swimming and playing the clàrsach

programme.title: Donnie Murdo (Danger Mouse)
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold celebrate another victory, gleefully ringing Big Ben's bell when Penfold falls, pushing the clock hand forward and causing the sun to go down prematurely

programme.title: Turas a' Bhradain (The Salmon's Journey)
programme.desc: Neen MacKay explores the salmon rivers of the country's northern reaches

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: A focus on dances of the world, including the A look at traditional dances, including New Zealand's haka

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Na Tiristich (From Tiree with Love)
programme.desc: The Tiree watersports club is a big hit with youngsters on the island, while Rhoda, Alastair and Iain take their lambs to the lamb sale and Tiree musicians take centre stage

programme.title: A' Sireadh Sascha
programme.desc: Always on Lachie MacPherson's mind is Sascha, the son he hasn't seen in 34 years. Now 60 and after recent major life events, Lachie is determined that, after years of searching, 2018 will be the year that he - and his daughter Mischa - find Sascha

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: A portrait of the Pakistani community on Lewis and Harris, whose story goes back 60 years, with 2018 seeing them establish their own mosque in Stornoway

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: Ina and Jessie are on holiday again and head for the mountains. In their thirst for new experiences they follow a sign that points to an exciting opportunity. However things turn out to be a little bit different from what they were expecting

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)
programme.desc: Iain MacKinnon presents a mix of hymns, psalms and faith stories. Mairi MacInnes sings Sheas a Mhàthair and Niall MacLennan and Alasdair MacPhee perform Psalm 42. Plus, Derek Murray shares the role faith plays in his life and Angela MacEachen reads from the Book of Proverbs

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Weasel tends to Fizz's toothache, but Magoo turns up, thinking he's busting into an animal testing lab.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The Spuds take the camper van on holiday, and end up at the bottom of the sea. Dave has to resort to a resourceful solution to get help.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: The GDF begin testing experimental rescue machines

programme.title: Dare Master at Home
programme.desc: A mega hopscotch course and a unique clothes competition are just two of the challenges facing Dare Master Danny and his competitors!

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr. Bean takes on the Bruisers at the local skateboard park, using only a mobility scooter.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mrs. Wicket becomes inconsolable when Scrapper goes missing. Mr. Bean investigates.

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Madcap slapstick and mayhem as the celebrated cat and mouse plot against each other

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Mavis uses a cursed code to accidentally unleash a video game on the hotel

programme.title: DC Superhero Girls
programme.desc: Ready to emerge from her father's shadow, Zee Zatara seeks fame and acclaim performing for audiences at the pier.

programme.title: How
programme.desc: Frankie, Vick and Sam challenge each other to answer questions beginning with the word How. Sam shows the team how to tuck into a tasty worm treat, Frankie explains how a mistake rewrote a famous fairy tale, and Vick shows Frankie how balloons go bang with nothing more than a cotton wool ball.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Teen Titans Go takes a comedic look at the superheroes' lives. Viewers will finally get the opportunity to watch what life is like for the super teens.

programme.title: Cornwall and Devon Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter walks through Cornwall and Devon, which boast more than 5,000 miles of footpaths, from the rugged rocks of Dartmoor to the picturesque port of Padstow. In the second episode, Julia walks five miles of the stunning north Cornish coast, from popular picturesque Padstow to the surfing hotspot of Trevone Bay. Along the way she experiences dramatic coastal landscapes featured in Poldark, before getting up close to a herd of Fallow Deer in the historic Prideaux Estate

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan welcomes ITN's Tom Bradby and Dragons' Den's star Deborah Meaden to Hampshire to enjoy brunch cocktails and celebrate champion racehorses

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare a colourful menu to banish winter blues. On the menu are pea, pancetta and chicken tray bake, baked eggs served with roasted tomato and potato bread, sea bass with preserved lemon potatoes, and a peach upside-down cake

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team create a new garden for an eight-year-old girl in Nottingham, despite the worst weather they have ever experienced. Tilly Sawford was severely scalded in a freak accident six years ago and continues to require treatment to aid her recovery, leading to the staff at her local hospital recommending the family have a safe garden created for them, with a bold colour scheme and surprise areas for all of the children

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. A birthday cake sets a house on fire, a toddler puts an iPad in a washing machine and a cow breaks into a home to use the swimming pool

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania 2 (2015)
programme.desc: Dracula is worried that his half-human grandson is not showing any vampiric tendencies, so enlists the help of his fellow monsters in showing the youngster how it is done. The count is shocked to receive an unwelcome visit from his own father, who does not approve of his son's tolerance of humans. Animated comedy, with the voices of Adam Sandler and Andy Samberg

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Suzi Perry, Mark Porter, Rosemarie Ford and Louis Walsh pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby welcome a dozen new celebrities who compete to become champions of the rink. This year's contestants - six of whom will perform tonight - are Billie Shepherd, Colin Jackson, Denise Van Outen, Faye Brookes, Graham Bell, Jason Donovan, Joe-Warren Plant, Lady Leshurr, Myleene Klass, Rebekah Vardy, Rufus Hound and Sonny Jay. Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean head up the ice panel alongside Ashley Banjo and John Barrowman

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Six more contestants have 15 questions, four lifelines and one chance to win £1million as the quiz hosted by Jeremy Clarkson returns. Donald Fear became the series' sixth jackpot winner in 2020, but it remains to be seen whether any of the latest players have what it takes to become the seventh millionaire

programme.title: Finding Alice
programme.desc: For Alice, Harry and their daughter, Charlotte, moving into their new home should have been a dream come true. But it turns into a nightmare when Alice discovers Harry dead at the bottom of the stairs in the property he designed. Drama, starring Keeley Hawes, Gemma Jones and Kenneth Cranham

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his son hit the road together again, but this time they are staying closer to home and heading for Europe as they travel from Essex to Italy's Amalfi coast. Their first stop is the Netherlands, where they try out the sport of canal jumping, before the pair don Lycra for a spot of velodrome track cycling alongside a world champion, and take on the world's tallest freestanding climbing wall

programme.title: The Pembrokeshire Murders: Catching the Gameshow Killer
programme.desc: Following on from the three-part drama, this documentary tells the real story behind the Pembrokeshire Murders. After murdering four people in Pembrokeshire, John Cooper eluded prosecution for 20 years until a new detective team and advances in forensic science finally saw him put behind bars in 2011. For the first time, the key people involved in the case reveal their role in bringing the serial killer to justice

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Adil Ray in which three pairs of contestants use their powers of deduction to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to win the money they've banked earlier in the show to potentially double their winnings, or leaving with nothing

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: A short bulletin rounding up the day's stories

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil action from Silverstone, featuring the Ginetta GT5 Challenge and the Mini Challenge UK

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team visit Aerospace Bristol, where Mark Stevens hits a slight problem when he attempts to land a pair of luxury watches

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Adil Ray hosts as married couple Lalaine and Steven, siblings Farah and Ibby, and flatmates Tom and Tom use their powers of deduction to try to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Elaine, Jenny, Judi and Che pit their wits against quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but if anyone is caught they're out for good, leaving fewer players for the final chase

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: A flamboyant public proposal puts pay to Paul's plan. Will is surprised how empathetic Kim is. Zak demands answers

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Faye confesses to Craig how Ray tried to rape her, while Mick chucks Crosby out of his van and tells him he has got 24 hours to find the money

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney continue their European adventure in Germany. They head to the city of Frankfurt, where Brad is pushed to the edge, literally, as he attempts to abseil down the side of a building, facing forwards. The boys also make a stop at a bratwurst-themed hotel and soak up some local Bavarian culture when they have a go at finger wrestling. The pair finish off their time in Germany with a wet and wild adventure in the mountains

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Dylan finally opens up to Sean and explains that he's being bullied at school because his dad's gay. In the police interview room, a terrified Faye explains how she attacked Adam Barlow thinking he was Ray Crosby

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell return to help more people reunite with long-lost relatives. Phyllis and Kevin Haran fought in vain to get their son back through the courts after putting him up for adoption when they were homeless, jobless and desperate to put their baby's interests first. The other story follows 64-year-old Michael O'Neil in his search for his birth mother

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Paul Sinha's TV Showdown
programme.desc: Chaser, stand-up comedian and TV trivia genius Paul Sinha hosts this TV-themed comedy quiz show. Team captains Fay Ripley and Rob Beckett are joined by celebrity guests Chizzy Akudolu, Shirley Ballas, Roman Kemp and Iain Stirling to compete head-to-head and test their TV knowledge

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley.

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: A short bulletin rounding up the day's stories

programme.title: Zone of Champions
programme.desc: Comedy clip show, in which a pair of sports commentators (played by John Thomson and Jessica Knappett) narrate home movie footage as if they were sporting events, including Hamster Athletics and Using an Escalator for the Purposes of Banter

programme.desc: David Dickinson helps members of the public in Crewe sort the treasures from their trash, including a cheeky chimp, a collection of autographs, and some modern art

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Kimberley, James, Niall and Nicola, who have never met before, pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paul breaks down and makes a big admission. Tracy is jealous. Kim enlists Mack's help in a plan

programme.title: South Africa with Gregg Wallace
programme.desc: The MasterChef presenter flies into Upington, the gateway to the Kalahari, and follows the Orange River to the spectacular Augrabies Falls. Paddling down river, Gregg stays overnight at Khamkhirri Lodge and has a traditional Afrikaans barbecue. He then heads into the heart of the desert where he faces a tough taste challenge and is surprised by a secret spring

programme.title: Martin Clunes: Islands of Australia
programme.desc: The actor travels to the Tiwi Islands, swims with a whale shark, and visits the Houtman Abrolhos, a string of over 100 little islands. Martin Clunes also samples life on Rottnest Island, which has long been a playground retreat for mainland visitors

programme.title: Outbreak: The Virus That Shook the World
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the spread of Covid-19 across four continents during 2020. Featuring contributions from medical professionals, a look at the initial outbreak in China and suggestions that the authorities attempted to cover up the danger, and an examination of how the decisions taken by governments and public health officials helped shape the course of the infection

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams narrates a compilation of TV clangers from the past year's programmes. Featuring live on-air blunders from Stormzy, Robbie Williams, Craig Revel Horwood, Jonathan Ross, and Ant & Dec, as well as a series of outtakes starring Will.i.am and Jeremy Clarkson. Plus, two toddlers run rings around Kay Burley, and Vicky Pattison forgets the name of the show she's presenting

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: A short bulletin rounding up the day's stories

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: As the makeover show returns, Alan Titchmarsh and the team head to Fareham near Southampton to surprise Betty Richards, a life-long volunteer. The 90 year old suffers from macular degeneration, and her garden is a green-free concrete box - with so many levels and trip hazards that it's off limits to the sight-impaired dare-devil fundraiser, so the team sets about transforming it into a safe haven

programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. programme.desc: Bradley and Barney continue their European adventure in Germany. They head to the city of Frankfurt, where Brad is pushed to the edge, literally, as he attempts to abseil down the side of a building, facing forwards. The pair finish off their time in Germany with a wet and wild adventure in the mountains

programme.desc: Documentary exploring the spread of Covid-19 across four continents during 2020. Prunella Scales guest stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part one of two. While holidaying in Cornwall with his friend Captain Hastings, the sleuth meets a local woman who has seemingly become unaccountably accident-prone - but soon starts to suspect she is actually a victim of foul play. David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A schoolgirl is found dead after a disco, and Cornish copper Charles Wycliffe applies himself to the task of discovering whether she was murdered or committed suicide. Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters star

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Young physiotherapist Lizzie Faulkner is shot dead while enjoying a holiday at a luxury country retreat. The resulting murder investigation unearths betrayal, guilt and the desire for atonement - as Vera moves quickly to avoid another tragedy, and Joe tries to make amends with the demons of his past. Crime drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, David Leon, Paul Ritter and Eva Quinn

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Sam and Foyle are shot at and almost run over by a speeding vehicle, and discover the incident is part of an illicit fuel racket. An undercover investigation reveals the detective's son Andrew to be involved, and the discovery that a driver at the petrol company has been murdered complicates matters further. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Julian Ovenden

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Ever since a beautiful American woman arrived in the peaceful village of Wychwood-under-Ashe, people have begun to die in a series of apparent accidents. Nobody suspects a killer is at work but, as the body count rises, Miss Marple digs a little deeper with the help of a former policeman - and they gradually unearth a secret that will rock the village to its core. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, Benedict Cumberbatch, David Haig, Sylvia Syms, Margo Stilley and Hugo Speer

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ashley worries that Maxine thinks he is a joke. Fiona and Alan set their wedding date. Kevin succumbs to Sally's demands and tells her he will try to find the money for her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Rachel asks Steve out on a date. Fiona tells Jim to stay away from her and Alan. Samantha hears screaming and finds Leanne being attacked by Mickey and Pete

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Barnaby launches a murder investigation when the severed hand of a missing hotel manager is found by a dog. The dead man had recently been named in the will of the hotel owner, and solving the case takes on a renewed sense of urgency when fatal accidents start happening to the other beneficiaries. Mystery, guest starring Rosemary Leach and Samantha Bond, with John Nettles and Daniel Casey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Called to investigate the attempted theft of a headless skeleton from an archaeological dig, Wycliffe finds himself forced to deal with landowners whose archaic values are jarringly at odds with the modern world. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The rural bliss of Ferne Basset is rudely shattered by the discovery of an elderly resident's body in the river. When Barnaby and Troy descend on the village to ferret out the culprit, they get caught up in simmering backstage rivalries at a local amateur dramatics group that Joyce has recently joined - and the situation grows more sinister when one of the actors is tricked into killing himself in the middle of a performance

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle investigates what he believes to be a burglary at the headquarters of a multinational company, and the murder of a Home Guard member during a large-scale exercise on the Sussex Downs soon leads him to believe the firm are conducting covert business with the Nazis - and that his former police sergeant may be involved. Second World War crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers take in a lodger, and Mildred is delighted when he begins doing all the household jobs that George will not tackle. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George is told to mind his table manners when Mildred invites her sister round for a meal. Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce star

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Peter returns from Scotland, and Daniel offers to be his liver donor. Simon tries to encourage Leanne to eat, and Dev tells Asha and Aadi that he's organised a surprise for their 16th birthday

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Sarah is missing, Danny offers her drugs, and when Zak returns he reinstates himself as head of the Dingles

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby visits his Aunt Alice, who is recovering from an operation at a nursing home, and finds himself in the middle of a mystery. A resident has been found dead and the official verdict is a heart attack, but as rumours of fraud and deception abound, Alice's friend believes someone may have conspired to commit murder

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster is invited to the wedding of her former housemaid Gwenda, only to be caught up in a murder mystery involving the groom's family. Geraldine McEwan stars, with Juliet Stevenson, Denis Lawson and Alison Steadman

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A petty thief is impaled with a pitchfork in sleepy Midsomer Mallow, jeopardising the residents' chances of winning a 'perfect village' competition. To make matters worse, two of the judges are also killed. Guest starring Timothy West and Hannah Gordon, along with an early appearance by a young Orlando Bloom as the first victim

programme.title: Inside the Ritz Hotel
programme.desc: Documentary offering a behind the scenes look at life inside the Ritz Hotel in London, one of the most famous, traditional luxury hotels in the world. The programmes follows some of the famous faces that visit the hotels, including Richard E Grant, Anna Wintour and Mo Salah, and the work of hotel staff as they prepare for their famous afternoon tea. With consumers spending less and ever more luxury hotels opening their doors, how does one of the most well-known hotels in the world stand out from the rest?

programme.title: Inside the Ritz Hotel
programme.desc: Executive chef John Williams prepares to launch the first-ever cookbook that reveals The Ritz's most famous recipes. Penny Lancaster pops in for cocktails, and head concierge Michael De Cozar is celebrated for his 46 years of service

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The investigation into the murder of 29-year-old Niall is made difficult by those closest to him, who seem intent on obscuring the facts, and it is not until the body of a drug dealer washes up on a beach that the dreadful truth begins to emerge. While coping with revelations of her own, Vera sifts through evidence of the grief, love and addiction that characterised Niall's life as she tries to find out why someone wanted him dead. With Judy Parfitt (Call the Midwife) and Phyllis Logan (Downton Abbey)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. Two of the missives have led to the deaths of Mona Symmington, the wife of Lymstock solicitor Richard, and Agnes Woddell, the parlour maid at the Symmingtons' house. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective applies his professional know-how to a fatal shooting, and soon begins to suspect that the dead man's brother-in-law may know more about the case than he is letting on - a theory borne out by forensic tests on the murder weapon. Jack Shepherd stars as the dour detective, with Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.desc: The year is 1940, and Sam is made homeless when a bomb destroys her boarding house, alerting Foyle to a system of organised looting around Hastings. Police drama, starring Michael Kitchen, with Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks

programme.title: The Car Chasers
programme.desc: Jeff and Meg realise that they need to hire another mechanic to expand the business and get a local Luke Cork to fill the role. Jeff comes across two nearly perfect highly customised 1962 Impalas that fit in with his and Meg's plans to grow. It is a catalyst for Flat 12 to attend a higher-end auction in Auburn, Indiana

programme.title: Auto Mundial
programme.desc: The latest news and reviews from the world of cars. Featuring a new concept car from MINI and the Ferrari SF90.

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil action from Silverstone, featuring the Ginetta GT5 Challenge and the Mini Challenge UK

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A racehorse trainer asks Holmes to investigate the mysterious disappearance of one of his cash-strapped employer's many creditors. Victorian whodunit, starring Jeremy Brett and Edward Hardwicke, with a young Jude Law in an early supporting role

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: Skipper Squizzy and Tabor are halfway through their five-day run. They have just 48 hours to hit their target - and then sell 500 kilos of lobsters direct to punters

programme.title: Extreme Salvage Squad
programme.desc: The world of marine salvage and rescue in Australia, filmed in the Whitsunday Islands off Queensland

programme.title: The Man from the Alamo (1953)
programme.desc: A soldier serving at the battle of the Alamo is sent to warn his comrades' families of the impending danger, but ends up falsely branded a deserter. He tries to clear his name with acts of heroism, including tracking down the traitor who robbed the casualties after the battle was over. Western, starring Glenn Ford and Julia Adams

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: Latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: The Man from the Alamo (1953)
programme.desc: A soldier serving at the battle of the Alamo is sent to warn his comrades' families of the impending danger, but ends up falsely branded a deserter. He tries to clear his name with acts of heroism, including tracking down the traitor who robbed the casualties after the battle was over. Western, starring Glenn Ford and Julia Adams

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: The hapless fool sits an exam, but when he realises he has studied the wrong maths equations, it is already too late. He also tries to stifle a sneeze in church. Rowan Atkinson stars in the first episode of the celebrated comedy, with Richard Briers, Rudolph Walker and Howard Goodall

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: The buffoon wreaks havoc when he goes shopping and dines at an exclusive restaurant - and there is an unfortunate incident when he meets a member of the royal family at a film premiere. Rowan Atkinson stars

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: The hero bumbles his way through a disaster-packed day that takes him from the swimming baths and lunch in the park on to a night at the cinema with his girlfriend. Rowan Atkinson stars, with Angus Deayton and Matilda Ziegler

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Rowan Atkinson leaves his brain at home once more to play the bungling buffoon of few words. He goes shopping for a new TV, has problems with an itchy foot and takes a lucky girl for a night on the town - leaving the usual trail of chaos in his wake

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: A routine trip to the dentist ends disastrously for Bean, and a quiet stroll in the park gradually turns into a pitched battle with a wasp. Rowan Atkinson stars, with appearances by Richard Wilson and Caroline Quentin

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Rowan Atkinson's clumsy alter ego returns to cause more chaos as he puts his dubious first aid skills to good use on an unconscious man in the street, makes a meal out of the simple task of posting a letter, and jets off for the holiday of a lifetime

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: The hapless hero plans the Christmas holiday of a lifetime - but fate intervenes and pulls the rug out from under his feet, reducing what should have been the perfect festive season to a frantic comedy of errors. Slapstick, starring Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Rowan Atkinson's comic alter ego embarks on another disaster-filled outing when he treats his hotel room to a makeover but ends up causing devastation

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: The bumbling hero tries his level best to turn a babysitting job into a fun-packed day at the fair, but predictably makes a hash of it. Slapstick comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: A New Year's party falls flat, but Bean finds an ingenious way to beat the sales crowds and bag a bargain before getting down to some seriously ham-fisted DIY decorating. Slapstick comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: An orientation day gives the hapless Mr Bean a chance to revisit his school days, and it is not long before he lives up to his disaster-prone reputation. Comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Rowan Atkinson slips into silly mode once more as his comic alter ego visits the launderette and somehow manages to lose his trousers, getting himself into a real pickle as he tries to retrieve them. Fully dressed again, he moves on to the crazy golf course, where the relatively simple task of hitting the ball leads to utter chaos

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Everyone's favourite idiot visits hospital to cure a nasty little problem, but proves to be a somewhat impatient outpatient, and later has trouble sleeping. Starring Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: The blunderingly inept master of disaster goes for a haircut, but ends up styling the other customers' hair. Later, he causes chaos at a school fun day. Comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Happy Birthday Mr Bean
programme.desc: How the sitcom character - who featured in just 14 original episodes - has gone on to become a global superstar with an animated series and two top-grossing feature films. Rowan Atkinson's alter-ego has become an online phenomenon, with more than 110 million online fans and growing. This documentary features classic clips and interviews with Richard Curtis, Tony Robinson and Angus Deayton

programme.title: Airplane! (1980)

programme.title: Smokey and the Bandit II (1980)

programme.title: Jeremy Wade's Mighty Rivers
programme.desc: Biologist, extreme angler and naturalist Jeremy Wade heads to the Amazon. Together with its tributaries, it contains an incredible 20 per cent of Earth's fresh water, and the immense forests it sustains generate a quarter of the oxygen needed for life on the planet. Disgorging more water than the next nine biggest rivers combined, this waterway is indeed a mighty affair. By tracing the epic migration of the dorado catfish, Jeremy discovers the issues putting this great river at risk - from leaking pipelines to giant dams. However, he also discovers tales of surprising hope

programme.title: The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes

programme.title: The Sweeney

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Mrs Peel is kidnapped by a mad Teutonic film director, who is attempting to make a film about her life. Unfortunately for Peel it ends with her death, leaving Steed in a hurry to save her. Patrick Macnee and Diana Rigg star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur makes a hasty agreement to stage a burglary after his tailor's staff go on strike demanding more pay. programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur must find a way of paying a £20,000 tax bill immediately, but fortunately Dave has a drastic solution - faking a drowning accident. programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: The hapless fool sits an exam, but when he realises he has studied the wrong maths equations, it is already too late. He also tries to stifle a sneeze in church. Rowan Atkinson stars in the first episode of the celebrated comedy, with Richard Briers, Rudolph Walker and Howard Goodall

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: The buffoon wreaks havoc when he goes shopping and dines at an exclusive restaurant - and there is an unfortunate incident when he meets a member of the royal family at a film premiere. Rowan Atkinson stars

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: The hero bumbles his way through a disaster-packed day that takes him from the swimming baths and lunch in the park on to a night at the cinema with his girlfriend. Rowan Atkinson stars, with Angus Deayton and Matilda Ziegler

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Rowan Atkinson leaves his brain at home once more to play the bungling buffoon of few words. He goes shopping for a new TV, has problems with an itchy foot and takes a lucky girl for a night on the town - leaving the usual trail of chaos in his wake

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: A routine trip to the dentist ends disastrously for Bean, and a quiet stroll in the park gradually turns into a pitched battle with a wasp. Rowan Atkinson stars, with appearances by Richard Wilson and Caroline Quentin

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Rowan Atkinson's clumsy alter ego returns to cause more chaos as he puts his dubious first aid skills to good use on an unconscious man in the street, makes a meal out of the simple task of posting a letter, and jets off for the holiday of a lifetime

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: The hapless hero plans the Christmas holiday of a lifetime - but fate intervenes and pulls the rug out from under his feet, reducing what should have been the perfect festive season to a frantic comedy of errors. Slapstick, starring Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Rowan Atkinson's comic alter ego embarks on another disaster-filled outing when he treats his hotel room to a makeover but ends up causing devastation

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: The bumbling hero tries his level best to turn a babysitting job into a fun-packed day at the fair, but predictably makes a hash of it. Slapstick comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: A New Year's party falls flat, but Bean finds an ingenious way to beat the sales crowds and bag a bargain before getting down to some seriously ham-fisted DIY decorating. Slapstick comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: An orientation day gives the hapless Mr Bean a chance to revisit his school days, and it is not long before he lives up to his disaster-prone reputation. Comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Rowan Atkinson slips into silly mode once more as his comic alter ego visits the launderette and somehow manages to lose his trousers, getting himself into a real pickle as he tries to retrieve them. Fully dressed again, he moves on to the crazy golf course, where the relatively simple task of hitting the ball leads to utter chaos

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Everyone's favourite idiot visits hospital to cure a nasty little problem, but proves to be a somewhat impatient outpatient, and later has trouble sleeping. Starring Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: The blunderingly inept master of disaster goes for a haircut, but ends up styling the other customers' hair. Later, he causes chaos at a school fun day. Comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Happy Birthday Mr Bean
programme.desc: How the sitcom character - who featured in just 14 original episodes - has gone on to become a global superstar with an animated series and two top-grossing feature films. Rowan Atkinson's alter-ego has become an online phenomenon, with more than 110 million online fans and growing. This documentary features classic clips and interviews with Richard Curtis, Tony Robinson and Angus Deayton

programme.title: The Motorbike Show

programme.title: The Avengers

programme.title: Minder

programme.title: The Motorbike Show

First, he returns to Cafe Hon in Baltimore, Maryland, where owner Denise Whiting was trapped in a nasty battle with the community and had threatened the locals with lawsuits. Next, the chef goes back to Chiarella's in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. When he first visited owners Tommy and Dina DeFino, the business was struggling so badly that Tommy had completely lost his confidence and wife Dina had lost her patience. On his final stop, Gordon travels to Leone's in Montclair, New Jersey, where Rose Leone was depending on her lazy son Michael to run the restaurant
