programme.title: World's Strongest Man 2017
programme.desc: Highlights of the British Open from the Doncaster Dome, where Eddie 'The Beast' Hall will be looking to take his fourth British title. Eddie made worldwide news with his record-breaking half-tonne deadlift in 2016. This year he is on a mission to take his fourth Britain's Strongest Man title in order to beat the record set by Geoff Capes

programme.title: World's Strongest Man 2017
programme.desc: Highlights of the Europe's Strongest Man event, which took place at First Direct Arena in Leeds, and featured Hafthor Bjornsson in action. Presented by James Richardson, with commentary by Colin Bryce and Danny Wallace

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Documentary using eyewitness testimony and footage from camcorders, CCTV and phones to examine traumatic, shocking, humorous and occasionally bizarre feuds between neighbours. Including an Army veteran who was attacked with a knife, and a Polish couple forced to call the police after their neighbour threw petrol over one of them and threatened to burn down their conservatory

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Steve and David were once good friends, but are now embroiled in an ongoing dispute that started with a simple off-the-cuff remark and soon spiralled into an all-out war over the ownership of a hedge. Also featured is a man who sent some workmates round to the house of a former mayor to collect money for unpaid building work, but ended up on the wrong side of the law himself when she complained to the police about his behaviour

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Stewart and Iain head to Manchester, where a landlord is owed £8,000 in rent arrears and has already called for police back-up. As tempers rise, the pair must find a way to handle a situation that is spiralling dangerously out of control. Meanwhile, Brian and Del chase £3,000 owed to a firm of letting agents, before tracking a debtor who was accidentally over-paid for a job. Plus, Stewart and Iain face an eviction that is set to test their patience

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul and Ben face a challenging eviction in London, where a vulnerable tenant in rent arrears has barricaded her front door, and the agents receive a tirade of abuse. Meanwhile, Brian and Del chase a debt of nearly £2,000 in Surrey, before trying to recover almost £3,000 in Northamptonshire. Finally, Steve and Ru head to Portsmouth to evict a young family for a landlord who wants her property back

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court Enforcement Agents Paul and Steve are sent to north London to evict a young Romanian mother who owes £6,000 in unpaid rent, but when they arrive, they discover a family home that is not quite what it appears to be. Meanwhile, in Kent, Brian and Alan turn up the pressure on a debtor who has admitted to owing more than £7,000, but claims he cannot afford to pay up - despite having a garage full of expensive tools. Later, in south-west London, Brian accompanies Delroy as he tries to recover a £400,000 debt from a residential property, and when the pair trace the defaulter to an upmarket cafe, the man in charge lies about his identity

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: One-off edition in which the High Court enforcement agents are caught in the crossfire of three bitter family disputes, including a debt-dodging man who pushes his panic-stricken mother over the edge. Plus, a family booted onto the streets at night by a ruthless aunt, and a man who won't engage with the agents and loses his car

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Amy tries to talk a bemused Terese into considering fresh designs for the Lassiters uniform, and Shane gets to work helping Amy out with new ideas

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Ziggy tries to change Dean's mind about turning himself in, but their exchange churns up a lot of old feelings. Tori is very excited about her dinner date with Christian

programme.title: Benefits Britain: Life on the Dole
programme.desc: The documentary examining the lives of individuals on the dole in the UK follows the stories of Roma people who moved to the UK and are now surviving on payments from the welfare state. Ion is from Romania and wants to make £40,000 from benefits to help him build a new house for his family back in his home village, while Katarina arrived from Slovakia seven years ago and has 11 children and 11 grandchildren living in and around an estate in Rotherham, South Yorkshire. Nottingham resident Viorel is 25 and lost both legs in an accident in his native Romania when he was seven, and now wants to pursue a career in the movies

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: A one-off edition of the documentary in which journalist Nick Wallis reveals how CCTV and technological advances are helping to ensure convictions. Nick visits Stafford to meet seasoned CCTV operator and ex-police officer Barbara, who helps catch a drug user after spotting him on camera. Plus, control room operative Chris is on the lookout for regular shoplifters in Woking, while mild-mannered Jo foils a gang of thieves in Huntingdonshire

programme.title: Countdown to Murder
programme.desc: The true crime series returns with the case of pharmacist Mitesh Patel. In 2018 he called police to tell them he had returned home to find his wife, Jessica, murdered. However, it was all a lie. Within weeks, Mitesh's double life as a gay man had become apparent to the police. Far from being a committed husband and pillar of the Hindu community, he had been plotting an affair with his lover in Australia

programme.title: Born to Kill?
programme.desc: The crimes of serial killer Dennis Nilsen, who was arrested in February 1983 after human remains were found in his household drains. It was later discovered he had liked to bathe and dress his victims' corpses, and was guilty of killing at least 12 young men in and around his home in the north London district of Muswell Hill

programme.title: Mystic Pizza (1988)
programme.desc: Three pizza parlour waitresses reach turning points in their lives, but help each other through their problems. Kat falls for a man whose kids she babysits while about to head off to university, her sister Daisy starts dating a man from a richer background, and their friend Jojo has cold feet about her wedding. Comedy, starring Julia Roberts, Annabeth Gish and Lili Taylor

programme.title: Entertainment News on 5
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: Mystic Pizza (1988)
programme.desc: Three pizza parlour waitresses reach turning points in their lives, but help each other through their problems. Kat falls for a man whose kids she babysits while about to head off to university, her sister Daisy starts dating a man from a richer background, and their friend Jojo has cold feet about her wedding. Comedy, starring Julia Roberts, Annabeth Gish and Lili Taylor

programme.title: Big Body Squad
programme.desc: Cameras follow three super-sized individuals whose weight keeps them trapped in their own homes. Weighing nearly 60 stone, Keith is a virtual prisoner in his bed, and even washing him presents a huge challenge for his carers

programme.title: Diet Secrets & How to Lose Weight
programme.desc: A team of scientists, doctors and dieticians reveal the benefits of superfoods, nutrient-rich stars of the culinary world that are said to have all sorts of weight loss and health-giving properties. Plus, Bianca Gascoigne swaps chardonnay for chia seeds to see what effect they have on her health

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Amy tries to talk a bemused Terese into considering fresh designs for the Lassiters uniform, and Shane gets to work helping Amy out with new ideas

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Ziggy tries to change Dean's mind about turning himself in, but their exchange churns up a lot of old feelings. Tori is very excited about her dinner date with Christian

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Columbo: Fade Into Murder (1976)
programme.desc: An actor who plays a detective on a soap opera is being blackmailed by his ex-lover, who is also his producer. Convinced that playing a sleuth makes him clever enough to get away with murder, he kills her and frames her husband - but has reckoned without the intervention of the crumpled cop. Crime drama, with Peter Falk and William Shatner

programme.title: Columbo: A Matter of Honour (1976)
programme.desc: The sleuth's holiday in Mexico is disrupted by a bizarre death involving a suave retired bullfighter, who has killed his best friend using one of the animals as a murder weapon - but is determined his spotless image and reputation will remain untarnished. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Ricardo Montalban, A Martinez and Pedro Armendariz Jr

programme.title: Columbo: Requiem for a Falling Star (1973)
programme.desc: A vicious gossip columnist digs the dirt on an ageing film actress, who responds by taking on her most challenging role yet - as a real-life murderer. But when the actress kills her own assistant in error, she looks for a way to shift the blame. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Anne Baxter, Mel Ferrer, Kevin McCarthy and multi-Oscar-winning costume designer Edith Head in a guest appearance as herself

programme.title: Columbo: Double Shock (1973)
programme.desc: A wealthy man due to marry a younger woman is electrocuted in the bath. Columbo suspects the victim's identical twin nephews - a flamboyant TV chef and a conservative banker - who stand to inherit his fortune if he died unmarried, but the detective is unsure which of them is the guilty party. Drama, starring Peter Falk, and Martin Landau in a dual role. With Paul Stewart, Julie Newmar and Jeanette Nolan

programme.title: Columbo Cries Wolf (1990)
programme.desc: The slovenly sleuth suspects the owner of a men's magazine is behind the strange disappearance of his business partner and former lover. The suspect insists the woman is still alive, while the detective struggles to prove his guilt owing to the absence of a body. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Ian Buchanan, Rebecca Staab, Deidre Hall and Alan Scarfe

programme.title: Columbo: Uneasy Lies the Crown (1990)
programme.desc: A dentist with gambling debts discovers his wife is having an affair with one of his patients and wants a divorce. He plans to take revenge by poisoning the lover with his wife's heart medication, so she will be suspected - but he reckons without the attentions of the crumpled cop. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, James Read, Paul Burke, Jo Anderson and Nancy Walker

programme.title: Columbo: Butterfly in Shades of Grey (1993)
programme.desc: A radio presenter is plotting to sabotage his adopted daughter's plans to publish her first novel and kills one of his employees for trying to help her. He thinks he has the perfect alibi, claiming that the victim was murdered while they were talking to each other on the phone, and there is a tape recording to prove it - but his ruse fails to fool the dishevelled detective. Crime drama, with Peter Falk, William Shatner and Molly Hagan

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of an ambitious architect with aspirations to design the new World Trade Centre - and discover he had a silent partner who may have been responsible for his murder. Crime drama, starring Vincent D'Onofrio, Kathryn Erbe and Jamey Sheridan

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A fugitive sex offender kidnaps his accuser, leading Detectives Benson and Stabler to question the man's dying mother to discover where he is heading. She believes her son is innocent and is merely trying to prove he is not guilty by forcing his 'victim' to go back on his lies. Starring Mariska Hargitay, Christopher Meloni and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Investigations reveal that the murder of a college student was linked to his role as pledgemaster of his fraternity, but Stabler and Benson are mystified when the murdered boy's father agrees to represent the accused. Drama, guest starring Serena Williams, with Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A waitress is found raped and murdered outside a nightclub, with suspicion falling on her ex-boyfriend. However, the detectives get a new lead when they discover the victim was in an abusive love affair with her boss, while news of her treatment is mistakenly leaked to her father - who vows to take revenge on the attacker. Joe Morton (Law & Order) guest stars, alongside Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A 14-year-old girl is found murdered, and as detectives investigate the case, it is revealed she is involved in the same charitable foundation as four abduction victims. The scheme is run by a prominent physician who specialises in hormone replacement and perpetuating youth. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Castle
programme.desc: The author returns to Los Angeles to find out more about the missing time related to his abduction, and The Greatest Detective Society requests his assistance in solving a murder

programme.title: Law & Order

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Police action to close down an illegal dog-fighting ring leads to a murder inquiry when officers find a woman's finger inside one of the creatures. programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A woman is murdered during an apparent road-rage incident, and the officers unravel a conspiracy involving arson. American crime drama starring Sam Waterston

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard try to solve the murder of a 17-year-old youth, and discover his younger brother had recently been banished from a polygamous Mormon sect. As they investigate the group's controversial leader, the detectives learn he had been trying to track down one of his wives. Crime drama, starring Jeremy Sisto, Anthony Anderson and Sam Waterston, with Colm Meaney (Star Trek: The Next Generation)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate when a man is crushed to death under a crane. Initially they suspect the construction company of foul play, but the case leads them to the victim's hospitalised wife, as well as plans for a controversial medical procedure

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Marine veteran confesses to the murder of a friend 60 years previously, but Gibbs decides to investigate further rather than take his story at face value, hoping to save the elderly Medal of Honour recipient from a spell in prison. Drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team arrest an ensign in connection with the death of a Navy commander who had recently undergone surgery, but Abby believes they have got the wrong man. Another fatality soon follows - and it falls to Tony to save his colleagues. Starring Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine's wife kills an intruder and later claims he tried to rape her. The team embarks on a mystifying wild goose chase through a stack of old case files and eventually discovers the woman was leading a double life while her husband was serving overseas

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony poses as a convict and takes part in a bogus jailbreak as part of an undercover operation to recover millions of dollars' worth of art and archaeological finds looted from Iraq - but disappears while manacled to a murderous fellow inmate. Guest starring Frank Whaley (Pulp Fiction), with Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

Drama, guest starring Jane Seymour, with Mariska Hargitay and Christopher Meloni

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

Starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson and Stabler race to find a woman with a high-risk pregnancy who has been kidnapped as the delivery date approaches. programme.title: Castle

programme.desc: Lupo and Green investigate when an adulterous art dealer is stoned to death. The detectives learn that the victim's plan to display provocative art led to unwanted attention from a terrorist organisation and gives them cause to suspect her son - a fanatical Christian - was persuaded by a religious leader to kill his mother. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston

programme.desc: Green comes under investigation by Internal Affairs after he shoots a suspect, and the case reveals a host of secrets the detective would rather keep hidden, threatening not only his police career but his freedom. Jesse L Martin stars, alongside S Epatha Merkerson and Linus Roache

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The officers leap into action when a financial planner is killed in her apartment building. It soon becomes apparent that the culprit could be a hit-man who was intending to murder someone else who happened to resemble the victim. Crime drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate the murder of a man visiting New York City to confront his past, but as they delve into the victim's personal life, the case becomes complicated when all clues point to a revered sheriff. Meanwhile, McCoy faces competition when a new district attorney is nominated for election

programme.title: Law & Order

Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

Jack is just months away from turning ten, the criminal age of responsibility, and is at risk of being locked up due to how badly he terrorises his dad. programme.title: The Listening Post
programme.desc: News. What is being reported by world media and how? Richard Gizbert guides you through the global news maze

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Series presented by Andrew White, featuring inspirational walks between 2-8 miles from around Britain

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White treks up The Great Orme in North Wales and beside a railway in West Yorkshire

programme.title: Callan
programme.desc: Spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent

programme.title: Callan
programme.desc: Spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent

programme.title: Charlton Heston Presents the Bible
programme.desc: The renowned actor serves as storyteller an ancient Roman amphitheatre and other locales in Israel provide the settings, bringing the King James text to life

programme.title: The Chiltern Hundreds (1949)
programme.desc: An aristocrat fails to be elected as a Tory MP, so decides to stand for Labour instead _ only to learn his butler, determined to maintain class distinctions, is running against him. Comedy, starring Cecil Parker, AE Matthews and David Tomlinson

programme.title: Bloomfield (1970)
programme.desc: An Israeli footballer nearing the end of his career is tempted to accept a bribe to throw his final game. A devoted young fan and a woman who is in love with him prick his conscience, but even if he chooses to do the right thing, his failing skills may not be enough to triumph over younger, fitter opposition. Sporting drama based on Joseph Gross' novel, co-directed by and starring Richard Harris, with Romy Schneider and Kim Burfield

programme.title: A Stitch in Time (1963)
programme.desc: A butcher's bumbling assistant causes no end of accidents at work, the latest of which has landed his boss in hospital. While visiting, he meets a little girl who has not spoken since her parents died, and resolves to put a smile on the orphan's face. Slapstick comedy, starring Norman Wisdom, Edward Chapman, Jerry Desmonde, Lucy Appleby and Jill Melford

programme.title: A Matter of Choice (1963)
programme.desc: A married woman finds herself caught in a moral dilemma when children inadvertently cause the death of her diabetic lover. Drama, starring Anthony Steel, Jeanne Moody and Ballard Berkeley

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Capital Conversation
programme.desc: Author and entrepreneur Michael Hayman interviews the business leaders at the heart of London's most exciting success stories

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Mike and Laura agree to go on a date, but chaos ensues in the restaurant when Laura loses a contact lens. Starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Laura buys a new dress for a special dinner with Ben. However, when her date reveals he is married, Mike happens by to pick up the pieces with his usual charm. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams as middle-aged romantics

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: Ash gets some time off and plans to spend it in bed - but Susan has other ideas

programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce explores the history of Windsor Castle, which has evolved over more than 1,000 years from its original role as a fortress and was nearly destroyed by a fire in 1992. Inside the residence is a musket ball that killed a naval hero, jewel-encrusted gold and silver table decorations, and Queen Mary's doll's house - complete with running water and a secret garden

programme.title: All You Need Is Love
programme.desc: Landmark series from the 1970s. This edition focuses on the story of glam rock. Featuring David Bowie, Alice Cooper, Jethro Tull and Elton John

programme.title: Priest of Love (1981)
programme.desc: Christopher Miles's dramatisation of author DH Lawrence's years in the US, Mexico and England, charting events following the banning of his novel The Rainbow to the writing of Lady Chatterley's Lover. Starring Ian McKellen, Janet Suzman, Ava Gardner, Penelope Keith and John Gielgud

programme.title: The Statue (1971)
programme.desc: A linguist's wife sculpts a nude statue of him to celebrate his achievements. However, when she grows weary of his neglect, she reveals that its impressive physique was modelled on another man. Convinced she has taken a lover, he sets out to identify the man behind the sculpture. Comedy, starring David Niven, Virna Lisi and Robert Vaughn

programme.title: Centrespread (1981)
programme.desc: A photographer in a dystopian future searches for a nude model he can turn into a star, a quest that leads him to question the society he has become a part of. Sci-fi drama, starring Kylie Foster and Paul Trahair

programme.title: The Comic Strip Presents
programme.desc: A postman is anxious to catch a morning showing of The Sound of Muzak at the local cinema. However, he is unaware a blue movie is actually to be shown and that his wife is its star. Comedy, written by and starring Rik Mayall and Adrian Edmondson

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Michael throws a fancy dress party at his house to celebrate the 33rd anniversary of the film Zulu, but the mood turns sour when the Monopoly board comes out

programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce explores the history of Windsor Castle, which has evolved over more than 1,000 years from its original role as a fortress and was nearly destroyed by a fire in 1992. Inside the residence is a musket ball that killed a naval hero, jewel-encrusted gold and silver table decorations, and Queen Mary's doll's house - complete with running water and a secret garden

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks along the cliffs at Flamborough Head and through Clumber Park, Nottinghamshire

programme.title: Pocket Dragon Adventures
programme.desc: A group of very small dragons live with a kindly old wizard, they're not big enough to fly but that doesn't stop them having many adventures

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: McMeer causes trouble after getting caught in Zigby's giant kite

programme.title: Zot the Dog
programme.desc: The tales of Zot, a good-hearted, fun-loving but rather bouncy dog and his best pal, Clive

programme.title: Skippy The Bush Kangaroo
programme.desc: The adventures of a young boy and his pet kangaroo

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Zeek's Animal World
programme.desc: Follow alien robot Zeek as he tries to find out some fascinating facts about our animal kingdom

programme.title: Skippy The Bush Kangaroo
programme.desc: The adventures of a young boy and his pet kangaroo

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks from Banavie to Gairlocky and around the Ribblehead Viaduct

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: As the destruction caused by Hitler's V1s in London and the south of England reached critical proportions, a new fighter was introduced to challenge the enemy's flying bombs. Wing Commander 'Bee' Beaumont remembers his first encounters with the Tempest, and the Wilkinson family from Croydon share their account of life on the ground with help from their day-to-day diary

programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce explores the history of Windsor Castle, which has evolved over more than 1,000 years from its original role as a fortress and was nearly destroyed by a fire in 1992. Inside the residence is a musket ball that killed a naval hero, jewel-encrusted gold and silver table decorations, and Queen Mary's doll's house - complete with running water and a secret garden

programme.title: Callan
programme.desc: Spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent

programme.title: Callan
programme.desc: Spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent

programme.title: Date with a Dream (1948)
programme.desc: Four wartime performers well known for their rousing concert recitals decide to reform and become a successful nightclub act all over again. Upbeat comedy, starring Wally Patch, Len Lowe, Terry-Thomas, Norman Wisdom and Jean Carson

programme.title: Rag Doll (1961)
programme.desc: A teenager runs away from her alcoholic stepfather to start a new life in London, only to fall under the influence of a seductive criminal, and get drawn into helping him rob her kindly boss. Crime drama, starring Christina Gregg, Jess Conrad and Hermione Baddeley

programme.title: I'm a Stranger (1952)
programme.desc: An Englishman comes to India to claim his inheritance, only to find his grandfather's will has gone missing. To discover what has become of it and acquire his rightful fortune, he needs the help of a detective, a window cleaner - and movie star Greta Gynt. Comedy, starring Patric Doonan and James Hayter, with Gynt playing herself

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing Richmond and Bellingham for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Danger Man
programme.desc: John Drake (Patrick McGoohan), a secret agent working for NATO, travels to different corners of the world in order to prevent crimes that threaten world peace

programme.title: Sapphire and Steel
programme.desc: The agents discover that Helen's bedroom is the location of a time break caused by the recitation of nursery rhymes

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: The consultants explain how changes in diet could help a 19-year-old dancer deal with Crohn's disease and boost the fertility of a man desperate to become a father. They also help a teenager redefine her relationship with food following gastric bypass surgery. Pixie McKenna examines the effectiveness of energy drinks, and a nationwide survey contributes to research into a natural remedy for insomnia

programme.title: After You've Gone
programme.desc: Comedy following the life of divorcee Jimmy Venables after his former wife leaves to perform charity work in Africa, prompting his mother-in-law to move in to the family home. Nicholas Lyndhurst and Celia Imrie star

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Featuring Lee, Bermondsey and Stratford. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh, a student at Kew in the 1970s, offers an insight into the work carried out behind the scenes by the 150 staff at the Royal Botanic Gardens, which house a diverse collection of plants and are a leading centre for scientific research and conservation

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Dorien may have ulterior motives for inviting Tracey to a posh event, while Sharon makes a tidy profit out of fake Rolling Stones T-shirts. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: National Treasure
programme.desc: Robbie Coltrane stars as Paul Finchley, one half of a much-loved comedy double act, whose life is turned upside down when he is arrested after an accusation of rape that dates back to the 1990s. Paul adamantly denies having even met the woman making the allegation and his loyal wife (Julie Walters) must decide whether to stand by him, while his troubled daughter (Andrea Riseborough) begins to question her relationship with her father

programme.title: Ladies That Punch
programme.desc: Documentary about the Ladies Boxing Association, whose members come from all walks of life, from catwalk models to IT workers

programme.title: Dagenham ()
programme.desc: A mother's fight against drugs, death and desperation in the deprived town of Dagenham

programme.title: Freehold (2017)
programme.desc: Fast-talking estate agent Hussein finds his personal and professional life unravelling as a wronged former client takes his revenge. Comedy horror, starring Javier Botet, Mandeep Dhillon and Mim Shaikh

programme.title: Felicity (1978)
programme.desc: A teenager in a remote Catholic girls' school seeks solace from her repressive life in reading erotic novels and having a secret love affair with one of her classmates. A trip to Hong Kong organised by her parents opens her eyes to more experiences - both good and bad. Australian drama, starring Glory Annen and Jody Hanson

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Series featuring inspirational walks between from around Britain. Andrew White walks on a disused railway in the Peak District and alongside the Caledonian Canal

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: How southerners reacted to the threat of Hitler's V1 flying bombs, which killed 452 people in a week at one point in the campaign. Notoriously inaccurate, the doodlebugs often fell short of their target and hit villages along the south coast from Kent to Dorset, prompting thousands in Chiselhurst to start living in caves

programme.desc: Comedy following the life of divorcee Jimmy Venables after his former wife leaves to perform charity work in Africa, prompting his mother-in-law to move in to the family home. Nicholas Lyndhurst and Celia Imrie star

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: A bomb is found in a nightclub which German scientists claim cannot be disarmed, and Ash receives a mysterious message. Guest starring Judy Geeson (the original 1970s Poldark)

programme.title: A Stitch in Time (1963)
programme.desc: A butcher's bumbling assistant causes no end of accidents at work, the latest of which has landed his boss in hospital. Slapstick comedy, starring Norman Wisdom, Edward Chapman, Jerry Desmonde, Lucy Appleby and Jill Melford

programme.title: Paper Tiger (1975)
programme.desc: An English tutor enthralls a Japanese ambassador's son with improbable fantasies of his heroic exploits, but then finds himself forced to act them out for real when he and the boy are both kidnapped. Comedy adventure, starring David Niven, Toshiro Mifune, Hardy Kruger and Ivan Desny

programme.title: After You've Gone
programme.desc: Comedy following the life of divorcee Jimmy Venables after his former wife leaves to perform charity work in Africa, prompting his mother-in-law to move in to the family home. Nicholas Lyndhurst and Celia Imrie star

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours for locals and tourists, exploring Elephant & Castle and Croydon. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: A 36-year-old man with gout visits the hospital, and despite the ailment's age-old associations with elderly men and rich living, he discovers his is hereditary. The doctors hear from a woman seeking a dietary cure for depression, and a mother-of-three who has lived on pancakes and chocolate for two years. Dr Pixie McKenna is also on hand to reveal the truth about detox diets

programme.title: Danger Man
programme.desc: John Drake (Patrick McGoohan), a secret agent working for NATO, travels to different corners of the world in order to prevent crimes that threaten world peace

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: The chef joins an unusual supper club that meets on an old Tube carriage, and makes sausage-and-mash for some taxi drivers

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Otway: The Movie (2017)
programme.desc: Documentary about the punk singer, following his career from the 1970s to the present day and offering his insights into how to survive in the music business

programme.title: Blue Iguana (2018)
programme.desc: Eddie and Paul, former prisoners working in a New York diner, are made an offer they cannot refuse by English lawyer Katherine Rookwood. Romantic comedy, starring Sam Rockwell, Phoebe Fox and Ben Schwartz

programme.title: The Weekend (2016)
programme.desc: Three friends stumble across £100,000, and spend it all on a shopping spree and a wild party. However, thwey then discover that the money belongs to a feared gangster - and he expects them to pay back every penny. Comedy, starring Joivan Wade, Percelle Ascott and Dee Kaate

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: The story of the havoc caused by the V1 doodlebugs and the V2 rockets, and of the thousands of deaths that undermined the morale of a war-weary civilian population

programme.title: Rag Doll (1961)
programme.desc: A teenager runs away from her alcoholic stepfather to start a new life in London, only to fall under the influence of a seductive criminal, and get drawn into helping him rob her kindly boss. Crime drama, starring Christina Gregg, Jess Conrad and Hermione Baddeley

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Featuring Lee, Bermondsey and Stratford. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White treks along the Isle of Man's stunning coast and visits Sprotbrough in South Yorkshire

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Laura buys a new dress for a special dinner with Ben. However, when her date reveals he is married, Mike happens by to pick up the pieces with his usual charm. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams as middle-aged romantics

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner

programme.title: British and Irish Lions Tour
programme.desc: Australia v British & Irish Lions. Action from the third and final Test at the ANZ Stadium in Sydney, as the 2013 tour reached its conclusion. The opening two Tests were both decided by the narrowest of margins - the Lions prevailed 23-21 in the opener in Brisbane, as Australia missed a chance to win the match with the final kick of the game, before the Wallabies earned revenge by triumphing 16-15 in Melbourne, with the visitors failing to score from a last-gasp penalty attempt on that occasio

programme.title: Fight Night
programme.desc: George Groves v Andrea Di Luisa. Another chance to see the bout for the vacant WBC International Super Middleweight title at the Copper Box in London. Home favourite Groves will have been hoping to bounce back immediately following his split-decision loss to Badou Jack for the WBC World title, which took place in September on the undercard of Floyd Mayweather Jr v Andre Berto in Las Vegas. A convincing win over Italian opponent Di Luisa would put Groves back on track for a fourth world title shot - potentially versus his old foe James DeGale, who holds the IBF belt

programme.desc: Paul Smith v Andre Ward. Another chance to see the bout from the Oracle Arena in Oakland, California. Unbeaten American Ward is the WBA (Super) super middleweight champion, but that title was not on the line in this catchweight contest against Liverpudlian Smith, whose last two fights were both points losses against hard-hitting WBO champion Arthur Abraham. programme.title: NFL: America's Game
programme.desc: A look back at New Orleans Saints' 2009 Super Bowl triumph over Indianapolis Colts at the Sun Life Stadium in Miami, Florida. Despite trailing 10-6 at the half-time interval, the Saints rallied in the second half to secure a 31-17 victory, claiming their first Super Bowl title in the process

programme.title: NFL: America's Game
programme.desc: The story of Green Bay Packers' 2010 Super Bowl success over Pittsburgh Steelers at the Cowboys Stadium in Arlington, Texas. This was the Packers' fourth overall win in the NFL's showpiece encounter, and their second since Super Bowl II under celebrated head coach Vince Lombardi, after whom the Super Bowl trophy is now named
